event_description: Iranian cyberattacks target US financial systems and Gulf state infrastructure: US Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) reports coordinated cyberattacks attributed to APT33 (Elfin) targeting US financial sector networks, Gulf state water treatment systems, and Israeli power grid control systems. Most attacks are mitigated. CISA raises threat level to 'Shields Up' — the highest alert tier.
timeline_data_point: CISA 'Shields Up' alert issued

event_description: President Trump addresses the nation: Trump announces Operation Epic Fury from the Oval Office. Calls it 'a decisive action to prevent a nuclear Iran.' Pledges 'no ground troops' and 'total air dominance.'
Oil markets surge: Brent crude spikes 18% in early trading as markets absorb the reality of a major Middle East conflict. Trading halted briefly on circuit breakers. WTI follows with 16% jump.
timeline_data_point: Brent +18% in first trading session

event_description: Minab girls' school struck — 165+ children and staff killed: US Tomahawk cruise missile strikes Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school in Minab, adjacent to an IRGC naval complex, during the opening wave at approximately 10:45 AM local time. 165-180 killed, mostly girls aged 7-12, plus teachers and parents. The school was triple-tapped per Iranian officials. Munitions experts confirm the weapon as a US Tomahawk. Pentagon preliminary investigation finds outdated DIA intelligence failed to distinguish the school from the military compound.
timeline_source: Iranian Red Crescent / NYT / CNN / BBC Verify / Bellingcat / OHCHR
timeline_data_point: 165-180 killed — deadliest civilian casualty incident

event_description: 6 US soldiers killed at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait: A projectile penetrates air defenses and directly hits a makeshift tactical operations center at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait. Six US Army Reserve soldiers from the 103rd Sustainment Command (Des Moines, Iowa) are killed. First US combat deaths of the conflict.
timeline_source: DoD / CENTCOM / CNN / Stars and Stripes
timeline_data_point: 6 KIA — deadliest single attack on US forces

event_description: Operation Epic Fury begins: US and Israeli forces launch coordinated strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities, IRGC command centers, and air defense networks. Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei killed in strike on Leadership House compound in Tehran.
timeline_data_point: 3,000+ targets struck in first 72 hours

event_description: Iran retaliates on Gulf states and Israel: IRGC Aerospace Force launches ballistic missile salvos at US bases in Kuwait, Qatar, UAE, and Bahrain. Simultaneous strikes on Israeli military targets including Nevatim Air Base and Ben Gurion Airport perimeter.
timeline_data_point: 200+ missiles launched in first wave

event_description: Iraqi militias launch rocket attacks on US bases in Iraq and Syria: Kata'ib Hezbollah and allied PMF factions launch coordinated rocket and drone attacks on Al-Asad Air Base in western Iraq, Erbil in Kurdistan, and Al-Tanf garrison in Syria. US CRAM systems engage incoming. Three US service members wounded at Al-Asad.
timeline_data_point: 3 US bases struck simultaneously

event_description: Iran launches Shahed drone swarms at Gulf military bases: IRGC launches waves of Shahed-136 one-way attack drones targeting US and coalition bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Qatar. Most are intercepted by Patriot, NASAMS, and CRAM systems. The volume of launches — estimated 150+ in the first 24 hours — strains air defense interceptor stocks.
timeline_data_point: 150+ Shahed drones launched in first 24 hours

event_description: B-2 Spirits lead opening strikes on hardened targets: B-2 Spirit stealth bombers flying from Whiteman AFB, Missouri deliver GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators on Fordow and other hardened nuclear sites. B-52Hs operating from Diego Garcia and Al Udeid launch cruise missile salvos at IRGC command centers. The air campaign involves assets from 3 carrier strike groups, multiple bomber wings, and F-35s operating from Gulf bases.
timeline_data_point: 3 carrier strike groups, B-2 and B-52 wings deployed

event_description: UN Security Council emergency session: Emergency UNSC meeting called. Russia and China condemn US strikes. US and UK defend action under self-defense and non-proliferation mandates. No resolution passed.

Saudi Arabia condemns both Iran and US strikes — calls for restraint: Saudi Arabia issues carefully worded statement through the Foreign Ministry condemning 'all acts that threaten regional stability' without naming either party. Riyadh privately grants expanded US basing and overflight rights while publicly maintaining distance. Crown Prince MBS speaks with President Trump and Chinese President Xi on the same day.

Iraq declares neutrality — closes airspace to US combat operations: Iraqi Prime Minister announces Iraq will not be a party to the conflict and orders closure of Iraqi airspace to US combat missions. The order is partially unenforceable as US forces maintain operational control over portions of Iraqi airspace. The declaration creates a legal and diplomatic crisis as US strikes on PMF targets violate the stated neutrality.
timeline_source: Iraqi PM office / AP / Al Jazeera

event_description: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed: Iran deploys naval mines and activates anti-ship missile batteries along the Strait of Hormuz. IRGC Navy fast attack craft patrol the waterway. Commercial shipping diverts. Lloyd's of London suspends Gulf transit insurance.
timeline_data_point: 20% of global oil supply route closed

event_description: Treasury yields plunge as investors flee to safety: 10-year US Treasury yield drops 28 basis points to 3.92% as global investors flood into US government bonds. The dollar index (DXY) surges 2.1% to 107.8 on safe-haven demand. Gold spikes to $4,850/oz. The flight to safety is the largest single-day move since the early days of COVID-19.

S&P 500 drops 3.1% — worst day since 2024: US stock markets sell off sharply as the full scope of Operation Epic Fury becomes clear. S&P 500 closes at 6,654, down 3.1%. Nasdaq falls 3.8% on tech exposure to global supply chains. Defense stocks (Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, Northrop Grumman) surge 8-12%. Airlines plunge 11%.

Oil surges further as Hormuz closure confirmed: Brent crude closes at $89.20/bbl (+7.2% from yesterday's already elevated close) as markets price in the Strait of Hormuz closure. WTI at $85.40. The combined loss of Iranian exports and Hormuz transit creates a theoretical shortfall of 12+ million barrels per day — the largest supply disruption in oil market history.

Pentagon confirms strikes on all known Iranian nuclear sites: DoD releases initial battle damage assessment confirming strikes on Natanz (centrifuge facility), Fordow (underground enrichment site), Isfahan (uranium conversion), and the Arak heavy water reactor. Natanz surface facilities assessed as destroyed. Fordow, buried under 80+ meters of rock, sustained multiple GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrator hits — damage assessment ongoing. Isfahan conversion facility severely damaged. Arak reactor building collapsed.
timeline_data_point: 4 nuclear sites struck — Fordow penetration uncertain

event_description: Iran's integrated air defense network assessed as suppressed: CENTCOM assesses Iran's integrated air defense system as effectively suppressed after 48 hours. S-300PMU-2 batteries at Isfahan and Tehran are confirmed destroyed. Indigenous Bavar-373 systems engaged but failed to intercept US stealth aircraft. Iranian Air Force F-14 fleet did not launch; their Mehrabad hangars were struck on Day 1. Remaining mobile SAMs and MANPADS continue to pose threats to non-stealth aircraft.
timeline_data_point: Iranian IADS suppressed within 48 hours

event_description: Houthis declare war on US and Israeli shipping in Red Sea: Ansar Allah (Houthi) military spokesman Yahya Saree announces all US and Israeli-linked vessels transiting the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb Strait are legitimate targets. The declaration extends the Houthis' existing Red Sea campaign from the Gaza war era to the new conflict.
timeline_source: Ansar Allah media / Al Masirah TV

event_description: India scrambles to evacuate 8 million citizens from Gulf states: India launches Operation Kaveri II — a massive diplomatic and logistics effort to prepare evacuation of up to 8 million Indian nationals across Gulf states. Indian Navy deploys three ships to the Arabian Sea. India calls for 'immediate de-escalation' but declines to condemn either side, seeking to maintain its oil supply relationship with both the US and Iran.
timeline_data_point: 8 million Indian nationals in Gulf states

event_description: Erdogan offers to mediate — condemns US strikes: Turkish President Erdogan condemns the US strikes as 'unilateral aggression that destabilizes the entire region' while simultaneously offering Ankara as a venue for negotiations. Turkey closes its airspace to US combat operations but continues allowing NATO surveillance flights. Turkey's position is complicated by its NATO membership, its economic ties to Iran, and the Kurdish factor.
Gas prices begin climbing across US: National average gas price rises to $3.25/gallon, up from $2.98 pre-war. Analysts project $4+ within weeks if Hormuz remains closed.
timeline_data_point: Gas +$0.27/gal in 48 hours

event_description: Second wave of militia attacks on US forces in Iraq: Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq claims responsibility for drone attacks on Camp Victory near Baghdad International Airport and a logistics convoy on Route Tampa. One US contractor killed. Iraqi government calls for 'restraint by all parties' but does not condemn the attacks.
timeline_data_point: 1 US contractor KIA

event_description: Hezbollah enters the war: Hezbollah launches massive rocket and missile barrage on northern Israel. Radwan Force units attempt cross-border incursion at Metula. IDF responds with airstrikes across Lebanon.
timeline_data_point: 500+ rockets launched at Israel in first 12 hours

event_description: 3 Israeli civilians killed in Hezbollah rocket barrage on Haifa: Three Israeli civilians killed and 22 wounded when a Hezbollah rocket barrage overwhelms Iron Dome coverage over Haifa. The volume of simultaneous launches — 200+ in a 10-minute window targeting northern Israel — exceeds the system's engagement capacity in the Haifa sector. IDF calls up additional reserve battalions.
timeline_data_point: 3 Israeli civilians KIA, 22 wounded

event_description: US Cyber Command disrupts Iranian military communications: US Cyber Command confirms offensive operations degrading IRGC command-and-control networks and Iranian military communications infrastructure. Operations are credited with slowing Iran's ability to coordinate retaliatory missile strikes. Details remain classified.

Japan and South Korea announce coordinated SPR releases: Japan and South Korea, both heavily dependent on Persian Gulf oil, announce coordinated Strategic Petroleum Reserve releases of 15 million and 7 million barrels respectively. Both governments support the US position but urge rapid de-escalation. Japan dispatches Maritime Self-Defense Force vessels to support Indian Ocean shipping escort operations.
timeline_source: Japanese PM office / South Korean MFA / Reuters
timeline_data_point: 22 million barrels combined SPR release

event_description: Markets stabilize as investors assess conflict duration: S&P 500 closes at 6,612, down modestly. Markets begin differentiating between sectors — defense and energy surge while airlines, travel, and consumer discretionary continue declining. Gold breaks $4,900/oz as safe-haven demand accelerates.

Brent crude hits $91.50 — third straight day of gains: Oil prices continue climbing as Hezbollah's entry and Houthi Red Sea escalation widen the conflict's geographic footprint. Brent closes at $91.50. Saudi Arabia and UAE pledge to increase production from non-Hormuz export terminals but capacity is limited to approximately 2 MBD via pipeline to Red Sea ports.
timeline_data_point: Brent $91.50. Saudi pledges 2 MBD via alternate routes

event_description: Houthi attacks on Saudi oil infrastructure: Yemen's Houthi forces launch drones and cruise missiles at Ras Tanura oil loading terminal in Saudi Arabia. Partial damage to loading platforms. Saudi Aramco reduces exports.
timeline_data_point: World's largest oil terminal struck

event_description: Global protests erupt: Mass anti-war protests in London, Paris, Berlin, Istanbul, Baghdad, and dozens of US cities. Estimated 2 million people march globally.
timeline_data_point: 2M+ global protesters

event_description: Satellite imagery shows Fordow survived deep strikes: Commercial satellite imagery from Planet Labs and analysis by the Institute for Science and International Security show that while Fordow's surface buildings and tunnel entrances are destroyed, thermal signatures suggest underground centrifuge halls may be partially intact. The site's depth exceeds the penetration capability of existing US bunker busters. Critics call this a 'strategic failure at the war's central objective.'
timeline_source: ISIS / Planet Labs / Arms Control Association
timeline_data_point: Fordow underground halls likely survived

event_description: Drone swarm overwhelms air defenses at Al Udeid, Qatar: A coordinated salvo of 40+ Shahed drones and 5 ballistic missiles targets Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar — the largest US air base in the Middle East. Most are intercepted but 3 drones penetrate defenses and strike the airfield, damaging a KC-135 tanker aircraft. No US fatalities. The incident raises questions about interceptor stockpile sustainability.
timeline_data_point: 3 drones penetrate Al Udeid defenses

event_description: First polls show narrow majority supports strikes — but opposition to ground war: An NBC/WSJ flash poll shows 54% of Americans support the initial strikes on Iran's nuclear facilities, but 71% oppose any ground invasion. Support breaks sharply along partisan lines — 82% of Republicans approve vs. 31% of Democrats. The school strike in Minab has not yet been widely covered at the time of polling.
timeline_data_point: 54% approve strikes, 71% oppose ground war

event_description: S&P 500 enters correction territory: US stock markets fall sharply as conflict escalation fears mount. S&P 500 drops to 6,297 — down 8.3% from pre-war close of 6,878.88. Defense stocks surge; airlines collapse. VIX spikes above 35.
timeline_data_point: S&P 500 6,297 — down 8.5% from pre-war

event_description: 14,000+ flights cancelled across Middle East and Europe: Airspace closures over Iran, Iraq, and parts of the Persian Gulf force massive flight cancellations. European carriers reroute around conflict zone, adding hours and fuel costs.
timeline_data_point: 14,000+ flights cancelled

event_description: Oil breaks $95 as conflict shows no signs of de-escalation: Brent closes at $95.30/bbl on the back of continued Hormuz closure, Houthi Red Sea attacks, and depletion of global crude inventories. IEA releases emergency assessment estimating commercial inventories can cover the shortfall for 45-60 days before critical shortages emerge in Asia-Pacific and European markets.
timeline_data_point: Brent $95.30. IEA: 45-60 day inventory runway

event_description: US strikes PMF weapons depots in Iraq: US conducts airstrikes on three Kata'ib Hezbollah weapons storage facilities in Jurf al-Sakhar and near the Iraq-Syria border. Iraqi Prime Minister condemns the strikes as a 'violation of sovereignty.' Iraqi parliament begins emergency session to discuss expelling US forces.
timeline_data_point: 3 PMF sites struck on Iraqi soil

event_description: Houthis strike container ship in Red Sea — crew evacuated: A Greek-owned container vessel is struck by two Houthi anti-ship ballistic missiles approximately 60 nautical miles southwest of Al Hudaydah. The ship sustains significant damage and the crew is evacuated. Maersk and MSC announce suspension of all Red Sea transits, rerouting around the Cape of Good Hope.
timeline_data_point: Major shipping lines suspend Red Sea transits

event_description: Oman begins quiet shuttle diplomacy between Washington and Tehran: Oman's Sultan Haitham initiates back-channel communications between US and Iranian officials — reprising Oman's traditional mediator role. Swiss Ambassador to Iran (US protecting power) facilitates initial contact. Discussions are preliminary and focused on humanitarian corridors and POW treatment rather than ceasefire terms. Both sides deny talks are taking place.
timeline_source: Diplomatic sources / Omani media / Reuters (sourced anonymously)

event_description: 450,000 displaced in Lebanon: UNHCR reports 450,000 people displaced in Lebanon as Israeli strikes intensify against Hezbollah positions. Refugee flows toward Syria and northern Lebanon overwhelm capacity.
timeline_data_point: 450,000 displaced

event_description: Iranian civilian death toll estimated at 400+: Iranian Red Crescent and state media report over 400 civilian deaths from US and Israeli strikes in the first week. Independent verification is impossible due to media access restrictions. Iran claims the number is significantly higher. Pentagon states all targets are military or dual-use and that civilian casualties are 'deeply regretted but not the objective.'
timeline_data_point: 400+ Iranian civilian deaths (Iranian figures)

event_description: 7th US service member killed — drone attack in Syria: A US Army soldier is killed by a one-way attack drone at a patrol base in northeastern Syria. The drone, attributed to an Iranian-aligned militia, struck a housing unit. This is the 7th US combat death of the conflict and the first outside the Persian Gulf theater.
timeline_data_point: 7 US KIA total

event_description: Iron Dome interceptor stockpile concerns emerge: Israeli media reports the IDF has expended over 1,500 Iron Dome and David's Sling interceptors in the first week of conflict — a rate that strains production capacity. The US expedites delivery of Tamir interceptors from existing stocks. A senior IDF officer warns off-record that at current rates, 'we have weeks, not months' of interceptor supply for the northern front.
timeline_source: IDF (background briefing) / Haaretz / Times of Israel
timeline_data_point: 1,500+ interceptors expended in 7 days

event_description: Iranian hackers deface US government websites and leak military emails: An Iranian hacking group calling itself 'Cyber Avengers' defaces several US municipal government websites and leaks a cache of emails allegedly from CENTCOM personnel. Pentagon states leaked materials are mostly unclassified and some appear fabricated. The information warfare dimension of the conflict intensifies.
Brent crude breaks $100/barrel: Oil strikes push Brent past the $100 psychological barrier for the first time since 2022. WTI at $97. Analysts warn of $120+ if conflict extends beyond 30 days.
timeline_data_point: Brent $101.50 — first time above $100 since 2022

event_description: Markets rebound on SPR release and limited ground war signals: S&P 500 recovers to 6,580 as the SPR release announcement and White House confirmation of 'no ground troops' provide modest reassurance. The rebound is driven by short-covering and bargain hunting in oversold tech names. Analysts warn the recovery is fragile and contingent on no further escalation.
timeline_data_point: S&P 6,580 (+4.5% from Mar 5 low)

event_description: US strikes Iranian oil infrastructure: In major escalation, US strikes Kharg Island oil terminal (90% of Iran's exports), Abadan refinery, and oil storage facilities. Stated objective: degrade Iran's ability to fund ongoing retaliation.
timeline_data_point: 90% of Iran's oil export capacity targeted

event_description: Week 1 summary — Operation Epic Fury by the numbers: After 7 days: 3,000+ targets struck in Iran. Kharg Island, Natanz, Isfahan, Arak confirmed destroyed. Fordow status uncertain. Strait of Hormuz closed. Hezbollah and Houthi fronts active. 7 US KIA. 3 Israeli civilians killed. 400+ Iranian civilian dead (unverified). 450,000 displaced in Lebanon. Oil at $101.50/bbl. S&P 500 at 6,580 (-4.3%). Gas $3.38/gal (+13.4%). Gold $4,980/oz.
timeline_data_point: Week 1 cumulative

event_description: US anti-war protests grow — 500,000 march in Washington: The largest US anti-war demonstration since 2003 draws an estimated 500,000 people to the National Mall in Washington, D.C. Simultaneous protests in 120+ US cities. The Minab school strike images have become the defining visual of the anti-war movement. Counter-protests supporting the troops draw smaller but vocal crowds in several cities.
timeline_source: AP / National Park Service estimate / Reuters
timeline_data_point: 500,000 in D.C. — largest since 2003

event_description: Iraqi parliament votes to expel US forces — non-binding: Iraqi parliament passes non-binding resolution demanding withdrawal of US forces from Iraqi territory. The vote is largely symbolic — a near-identical resolution passed in January 2020 after the Soleimani strike and was never enforced. Iraqi PM declines to sign an executive order. Militia attacks on US bases continue at a rate of 2-3 per day.
timeline_data_point: Non-binding expulsion vote passes

event_description: Strategic Petroleum Reserve release authorized: President Trump authorizes release of 30 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve over 60 days. Critics note this replaces less than 3 days of Hormuz flow.
timeline_data_point: 30M barrels authorized

event_description: US strikes Houthi missile sites in Yemen: US Navy and Air Force assets strike Houthi anti-ship missile launch sites, drone storage facilities, and radar installations in Sana'a, Hodeidah, and Dhamar governorates. Houthis vow retaliation and launch drone swarm toward Eilat, Israel within hours.
timeline_data_point: 12 Houthi sites struck

event_description: IAEA loses all monitoring access to Iranian nuclear sites: International Atomic Energy Agency Director General confirms total loss of monitoring and verification access across all Iranian nuclear facilities. Inspectors were evacuated before the strikes. Iran announced withdrawal from the NPT on March 2. IAEA states it 'cannot assess the current status of Iran's nuclear material inventory.' The war intended to prevent an Iranian bomb may have made verification of that goal impossible.
timeline_data_point: IAEA monitoring access: zero

event_description: Mojtaba Khamenei named Supreme Leader: Iran's Assembly of Experts names Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the killed Supreme Leader, as the new Supreme Leader. Mojtaba vows 'resistance until victory' and rejects any ceasefire.

Global supply chain disruption spreads beyond energy: Disruption from Hormuz closure and Red Sea rerouting cascades beyond energy markets. Petrochemical feedstock shortages force production cuts at European chemical plants. Semiconductor-grade neon (produced partly in the Middle East supply chain) faces spot shortages. The Baltic Dry Index spikes 35% as available shipping capacity is absorbed by longer routes. Auto manufacturers warn of production slowdowns within 2-3 weeks.
timeline_data_point: Baltic Dry Index +35%

event_description: US air campaign targets Iranian drone production and storage: In a dedicated strike package, US forces target Iran's drone production infrastructure including facilities in Isfahan, Kermanshah, and a suspected underground Shahed assembly plant near Karaj. Defense Intelligence Agency estimates Iran began the conflict with 3,000+ one-way attack drones in stockpile. An unknown number remain operational despite strikes.
timeline_data_point: 3,000+ drone pre-war stockpile — remaining unknown

event_description: Red Sea shipping insurance premiums triple: War risk insurance premiums for Red Sea and Gulf of Aden transits triple to 2.5% of cargo value — up from 0.8% pre-war. Combined with Hormuz closure, approximately 35% of global seaborne oil trade is now disrupted or rerouted. Freight rates for Asia-Europe routes surge 40%.
timeline_data_point: 35% of seaborne oil trade disrupted

event_description: University of Michigan consumer sentiment plunges: University of Michigan preliminary consumer sentiment index drops to 62.1, down from 71.8 — the steepest single-period decline since March 2020. The decline is driven by expectations of higher gas and grocery prices. Americans cite the war as the primary reason for economic pessimism. Grocery chains begin implementing 5-8% price increases on imported goods.
timeline_data_point: Consumer sentiment 62.1, down from 71.8

event_description: Iranian infrastructure damage assessment — power, water, fuel: The ICRC reports significant damage to Iranian civilian infrastructure including power generation (30-40% of national grid offline in western Iran), water treatment disruption in Ahvaz and Kermanshah, and fuel shortages as refinery capacity is degraded. Hospitals are operating on backup generators. Iran's road and rail network is severely disrupted by strikes on bridges and logistics hubs.
timeline_data_point: 30-40% of western Iran power grid offline

event_description: Day 11 — War continues with no ceasefire in sight: Strikes continue across Iran. Hezbollah front intensifies. Oil at $101.50. S&P 500 recovers to 6,694 — rebounding 6.3% from last week's 6,297 low on SPR release, no-ground-war pledges, and short-covering. Markets remain 2.5% below pre-war 6,878.88. VIX holds above 25. Gas national average at $3.48 and climbing. Estimated US operational cost: $500M/day.
timeline_data_point: Estimated total cost: $5B+

event_description: Trump sends mixed signals on war's end: President Trump tells CBS News the war is 'pretty much complete' but later tells House Republicans 'we haven't won enough.' Demands 'unconditional surrender' from Iran. Mixed messaging roils markets and confuses diplomatic channels.

Oil crashes after Energy Secretary's false Hormuz claim: Energy Secretary Chris Wright posts on social media that the US Navy has escorted a tanker through the Strait of Hormuz. The claim is false — no escort occurred. Brent crude plunges 11.3% to $87.80 before partially recovering. The incident raises questions about information discipline within the administration during wartime.
timeline_data_point: Brent -11.3% in single session

event_description: Gas prices hit $3.63/gallon despite oil retreat: National average gas price rises to $3.63/gallon even as crude prices retreat. AAA notes gas prices lag oil by 7-10 days. California hits $4.81/gal. Analysts project continued climb as refinery capacity remains constrained by regional disruption.
timeline_data_point: $3.63/gal — +21.8% since pre-war

event_description: Largest militia barrage yet — 40+ rockets at Al-Asad: PMF-aligned groups launch the largest single barrage of the conflict at Al-Asad Air Base — over 40 rockets and 5 one-way attack drones. US air defenses intercept most, but several impact the airfield, damaging two MQ-9 Reapers on the ground. Two US service members wounded. CENTCOM warns of 'decisive response.'
timeline_data_point: 40+ rockets, 2 drones destroyed on ground

event_description: Three ships hit in Strait of Hormuz and Persian Gulf: UK Navy reports three vessels struck by suspected projectiles: Thai-flagged bulk carrier Mayuree Naree in the Strait off Oman, a container vessel west of Ras Al-Khaimah, and a bulk carrier northwest of Dubai. Attacks come after a 72-hour lull in maritime targeting.
timeline_data_point: 3 vessels struck in single day

event_description: US death toll rises to 8: Pentagon confirms an eighth US service member has died from injuries sustained during an Iranian missile attack on US troops stationed at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia on March 1. Sgt. Benjamin Pennington had been in critical condition since Day 2.
timeline_data_point: 8 US KIA total (7 enemy fire + 1 died of wounds)

event_description: Iran fires fresh missile barrage at Israel: Rocket trails seen over Netanya as Iran launches overnight ballistic missile salvo at Israeli territory. No direct impacts or injuries reported. Arrow and Iron Dome systems intercept all incoming. Fourth major Iranian salvo since Day 1.
timeline_data_point: All missiles intercepted

event_description: IDF strikes Hezbollah command centers in Beirut suburbs: Israeli airstrikes target Hezbollah command centers and weapons sites in Dahieh (southern Beirut suburbs). Four killed in strike on central Beirut apartment linked to Hezbollah operations. Lebanese displaced now exceeds 700,000.
timeline_data_point: 700,000+ displaced in Lebanon

event_description: NATO shoots down Iranian ballistic missile over Turkey: Turkey confirms NATO air defenses shot down a ballistic missile in Turkish airspace fired from Iran — the first such incident in NATO history. The missile was likely targeting Israeli assets or a wayward retaliatory launch. Turkey had previously deployed a Patriot system to protect a NATO radar station in eastern Turkey.
timeline_data_point: First NATO intercept of Iranian missile

event_description: Cyberattack disrupts Iranian oil export tracking systems: Iranian state media reports a cyberattack has disrupted the National Iranian Oil Company's logistics and tracking systems. Though Iran's oil export infrastructure is already physically destroyed, the attack complicates coordination of remaining domestic fuel distribution. Attribution unclear — likely US or Israeli.

NATO invokes Article 4 consultations over Turkey missile incident: NATO convenes emergency Article 4 consultations after Turkey's request following the intercept of an Iranian ballistic missile in Turkish airspace. While stopping short of Article 5 collective defense, allies agree to deploy additional Patriot batteries to southeastern Turkey and upgrade AWACS coverage. Iran warns Turkey of 'severe consequences' for 'siding with the aggressors.'
timeline_data_point: First Article 4 invocation over direct Iranian threat

event_description: Bipartisan group of senators demands AUMF vote: A bipartisan group of 34 senators — 18 Democrats and 16 Republicans — sends a letter to Senate leadership demanding a vote on Authorization for Use of Military Force within 72 hours. Senator Tim Kaine calls the war 'unconstitutional' without Congressional authorization. White House cites the 2001 AUMF and Article II powers.
timeline_data_point: 34 senators demand war authorization vote

event_description: China proposes 72-hour ceasefire at UNSC — US vetoes: China introduces a UN Security Council resolution calling for an immediate 72-hour humanitarian ceasefire and negotiations. Russia co-sponsors. France and UK abstain. US vetoes, stating it will not accept conditions that allow Iran to 'reconstitute its nuclear program or rearm Hezbollah.' Ambassador Linda Thomas-Greenfield calls the resolution 'a shield for Iranian aggression.'
timeline_data_point: US vetoes ceasefire resolution

event_description: Oil rebounds as markets reassess fundamentals: Brent crude surges back above $100 to close at $100.11/bbl (intraday range $92.89–$101.53) as traders recognize that yesterday's crash was driven by a false claim, not a genuine change in supply conditions. The Strait of Hormuz remains closed, 1,000+ ships remain stranded, and the 12.35 MBD shortfall persists. WTI closes at $95.28 (+9.2%). Gold breaks $5,126/oz on safe-haven demand.
timeline_data_point: Brent $100.11 — back above $100. WTI $95.28. Gold $5,127/oz

event_description: Gas prices climb to $3.72/gallon — California breaks $5.00: National average gas price reaches $3.72/gallon, continuing its relentless climb despite yesterday's oil price dip. California becomes the first state to break $5.00/gallon since the war began, hitting $5.02. AAA warns that the oil rebound today means no near-term pump relief. Trucking industry warns of freight surcharges that will ripple through consumer goods prices.
timeline_data_point: $3.72/gal — +24.8% since pre-war. CA breaks $5.00

event_description: Iranian state media claims hospital hit in Ahvaz: Iranian state television broadcasts footage of a damaged hospital in Ahvaz, claiming a US airstrike struck the facility, killing 12 patients and staff. Pentagon states the target was an IRGC logistics hub 400 meters away and that the hospital damage was caused by secondary explosions. Independent verification not yet possible.
timeline_data_point: 12 killed per Iranian claims — under investigation

event_description: Iranian civilian toll rises past 800 — refugee flows to Turkey and Iraq begin: Iranian authorities report over 800 civilian deaths. UNHCR reports early refugee flows — approximately 15,000 Iranians have crossed into Turkey's Van Province and an estimated 8,000 into Iraqi Kurdistan. Turkey warns it cannot absorb a mass displacement event and begins reinforcing its eastern border.
timeline_data_point: 800+ Iranian civilian dead. 23,000+ refugees

event_description: IDF launches ground incursion into southern Lebanon: Israeli armored and infantry units cross the Blue Line into southern Lebanon in what the IDF describes as a 'limited ground operation' to push Hezbollah back from the border. Golani Brigade and 7th Armored Brigade spearhead the advance toward the Litani River. Heavy fighting reported near Marjayoun. Hezbollah's Radwan Force engages with ATGMs, destroying at least 2 Merkava tanks.
timeline_data_point: First Israeli ground operation in Lebanon since 2006

event_description: Senate begins heated AUMF debate: The US Senate takes up the Authorization for Use of Military Force resolution demanded by 34 senators. Floor debate is heated — supporters argue the war is unconstitutional without explicit Congressional authorization; opponents call the debate 'aiding the enemy during wartime.' White House issues statement reaffirming Article II authority. Vote expected within 24 hours. Markets watch closely — an AUMF rejection could force a withdrawal timeline.
timeline_data_point: AUMF floor debate begins — vote expected March 14

event_description: Russia introduces rival ceasefire resolution at UNSC: Russia introduces its own ceasefire resolution at the UN Security Council, calling for an immediate cessation of hostilities, withdrawal of all foreign forces from Iranian territory, and an international conference on Persian Gulf security. Unlike China's vetoed resolution, Russia's draft includes language condemning 'unilateral military aggression.' US, UK, and France expected to veto. Diplomatic paralysis at the UNSC deepens.
timeline_data_point: Second ceasefire resolution in 48 hours — US veto expected

event_description: EU announces sanctions package on IRGC and Iranian oil: The European Union announces a comprehensive sanctions package targeting IRGC leadership, Iranian oil exports, and entities supporting Iran's ballistic missile program. The EU simultaneously calls for an 'immediate humanitarian pause' and offers to mediate. Iran responds by recalling its ambassadors from France, Germany, and the Netherlands. The sanctions signal Europe is aligning with the US position while maintaining diplomatic distance.
timeline_data_point: EU sanctions on IRGC + Iranian oil — Iran recalls 3 ambassadors

event_description: Oil eases slightly — Brent to $98.82 as convoy success tempers fears: Brent crude dips 1.3% to $98.82 as the second successful convoy transit and destruction of IRGC coastal batteries provide modest reassurance. WTI holds flat at $95.28. Analysts caution that the psychological impact of convoys exceeds the material impact — 5 tankers cannot replace 50. The fundamental shortfall of 12.35 MBD persists. Gold eases to $5,080/oz.

S&P 500 closes at 6,672.62 — slight decline on continued uncertainty: Markets drift lower as the AUMF debate introduces political uncertainty. S&P 500 closes at 6,672.62, down 0.3% on the day and 3.0% below pre-war levels. Defense stocks remain elevated at +16.5%. Oil majors ease 1% with crude. Airlines extend losses to -24.5% as fuel cost fears mount. VIX holds at 27.5 — elevated but below crisis peaks.

Gas prices hit $3.82/gallon — trucking surcharges take effect: National average gas price climbs to $3.82/gallon — up 28.2% from pre-war $2.98. California hits $5.15/gallon. The trucking industry's war surcharges announced yesterday take effect today, adding 8-12% to freight costs. Grocery chains warn of price increases on perishables within 3-5 days. The war's cost is no longer abstract — it is arriving at every checkout counter in America.
timeline_source: AAA / GasBuddy / American Trucking Associations / YCharts
timeline_data_point: $3.82/gal — +28.2% since pre-war. CA at $5.15

event_description: Displaced in Lebanon surges past 850,000: UNHCR reports displaced persons in Lebanon have surged past 850,000 as IDF ground operations enter their second day. Humanitarian corridors from southern Lebanon are overwhelmed. At least 65 Lebanese killed today, bringing the total to 773+, including 102 children. Medical facilities in Tyre and Sidon are operating beyond capacity. ICRC requests immediate humanitarian access for trapped civilians south of the Litani River.
timeline_source: UNHCR / OCHA / ICRC / Lebanese Health Ministry
timeline_data_point: 850,000+ displaced — 773+ killed including 102 children

event_description: US retaliatory strikes destroy IRGC coastal batteries: US strikes hit IRGC coastal missile batteries and fast-attack-craft pens at Bandar Abbas, Qeshm Island, and three additional sites along the Hormuz coastline. Pentagon calls it a 'proportional response to ensure freedom of navigation.' Iran's ability to threaten the strait corridor is degraded but not eliminated — mobile launchers remain operational.
timeline_data_point: 5 IRGC coastal sites destroyed

event_description: Houthis launch cruise missile at US destroyer in Red Sea: Houthis fire a land-attack cruise missile at USS Gravely (DDG-107) operating in the southern Red Sea. The missile is intercepted by SM-2. A second salvo of 3 one-way attack drones is shot down by CIWS. No US casualties. The Red Sea front now requires continuous carrier strike group presence, straining the Navy's two-theater deployment.
timeline_data_point: 1 cruise missile + 3 drones intercepted

event_description: IDF ground forces advance 5km into Lebanon — heavy fighting at Bint Jbeil: IDF ground forces push 5km north of the Blue Line into southern Lebanon on the second day of ground operations. Heavy fighting erupts near Bint Jbeil, a Hezbollah stronghold that was the scene of intense combat in 2006. Hezbollah's Radwan Force employs IEDs, anti-tank missiles, and tunnel positions. Six additional IDF soldiers killed — bringing the total to 10 since the ground incursion began. Hezbollah fires 300+ rockets at northern Israel in retaliation.
timeline_source: IDF / Al Jazeera / Hezbollah media office
timeline_data_point: 6 more IDF KIA — 10 total in ground operation

event_description: France and Italy open diplomatic talks with Iran: French and Italian foreign ministries announce preliminary diplomatic contacts with Iranian officials through Omani intermediaries. The talks represent the first direct Western diplomatic engagement since the war began. EU sanctions on IRGC and Iranian oil announced alongside the outreach. Iran had recalled ambassadors from France, Germany, and the Netherlands on March 13.
timeline_data_point: First Western diplomatic contact since war began

event_description: Gas reaches $3.675/gallon — AAA confirmed: AAA reports national average gasoline price at $3.675 per gallon, confirmed via OPIS credit card transaction data from ~130,000 stations. This represents a $0.695 increase (+23.3%) from the pre-war baseline of $2.98. The EIA Short-Term Energy Outlook (March 10) had forecast a March average of $3.58 — a figure already exceeded by mid-month.
timeline_data_point: $3.675/gal — +23.3% since pre-war $2.98

event_description: Iran Health Ministry: 1,444 killed, 18,551 injured: Iranian Health Ministry releases updated casualty figures: 1,444 killed and 18,551 injured since February 28. Separately, Hengaw (Kurdish human rights organization) reports 4,400+ Iranian military personnel killed, a significantly higher figure. Iranian Red Crescent reports 20,000+ civilian buildings affected including 16,000 residential units. 31 major hospitals damaged, 12 rendered inactive. WHO has verified 13 attacks on healthcare infrastructure in Iran.
timeline_source: Iranian Health Ministry / Hengaw / Iranian Red Crescent / WHO
timeline_data_point: 1,444 killed + 18,551 injured (Health Min.) — 4,400+ military (Hengaw)

event_description: Qatar intercepts 4 ballistic missiles — first evacuation orders in Doha: Qatar Defense Ministry intercepts 4 ballistic missiles and several drones targeting Doha. Large blast visible over Doha at approximately 1:30 AM from Patriot intercept. First evacuation orders issued in some Doha city districts. All incoming projectiles neutralized. Qatar has been targeted continuously since Feb 28 but this represents the heaviest single-night attack on the capital.
timeline_data_point: 4 ballistic missiles + several drones — all intercepted

event_description: 2,500 Marines ordered to Middle East — USS Tripoli deployment: The 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit (2,500 Marines) aboard USS Tripoli (amphibious assault ship) is ordered to the Middle East, pulled from waters near Taiwan. USS Nimitz deployment also extended. The Marine deployment signals preparation for potential Strait of Hormuz escort operations planned for late March. This represents a significant reallocation of Pacific-focused naval assets to the Persian Gulf theater.
timeline_data_point: 2,500 Marines (31st MEU) + USS Tripoli redeployed from Pacific

event_description: Fujairah oil hub fire — intercepted drone debris strikes bunkering facility: Debris from an intercepted Iranian drone strikes the major Fujairah oil bunkering hub in the UAE, starting a fire. One Jordanian citizen lightly injured. Oil-loading operations suspended at the facility. Fujairah is a critical alternative to Hormuz-dependent ports for oil exports via the Habshan-Fujairah pipeline.
timeline_data_point: Fujairah bunkering operations suspended

event_description: Ahmed al-Jaber Air Base struck — 3 Kuwaiti soldiers injured: Two Iranian drones strike Ahmed al-Jaber Air Base in Kuwait. Three Kuwaiti soldiers injured. Of 7 drones detected targeting the base, 3 were intercepted and 2 fell outside the threat zone. Kuwait has been targeted continuously since Day 1, with Ali al-Salem and Camp Buehring also receiving regular attacks.
timeline_data_point: 2 of 7 drones struck base — 3 soldiers injured

event_description: US strikes 90+ military targets on Kharg Island — oil infrastructure spared: CENTCOM executes large-scale precision strike on Kharg Island, targeting 90+ military objectives including naval mine storage, missile storage bunkers, air defense facilities, naval base, airport, and helicopter hangar. Oil infrastructure deliberately preserved. Trump warns he will 'reconsider' sparing oil assets if Iran continues blocking the Strait of Hormuz. Trump called it 'one of the most powerful bombing raids in the History of the Middle East.' Iran FM vows retaliation.
timeline_source: CENTCOM / Trump Truth Social / NPR / CNN / Washington Post
timeline_data_point: 90+ military targets struck — oil infrastructure deliberately spared

event_description: Trump threatens to hit Kharg Island again — urges allied warships to Strait: President Trump states he may strike Kharg Island again 'just for fun' and urges allied nations to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian FM vows retaliation for the March 13 Kharg strikes. The USS Nimitz deployment is extended. The rhetoric signals continued US pressure on Iran to reopen the Strait while the military prepares for escort operations in late March.
timeline_data_point: Trump threatens additional Kharg strikes; urges allied naval deployment

event_description: Estimated war cost reaches $20 billion — conflict enters third week: Extrapolating from the Pentagon's confirmed $11.3B through Day 6 and CSIS's $16.5B through Day 12 at a daily run rate of approximately $850M, cumulative direct military costs are estimated to have reached approximately $20 billion as of Day 16. Penn Wharton projects total direct budgetary costs of $40-95 billion (central estimate ~$65B) if the conflict lasts two months. The Pentagon is preparing a ~$50B supplemental funding request. Additional US economic losses from energy disruption estimated at $50-210B by Penn Wharton.
timeline_source: Pentagon / CSIS (Cancian & Park) / Penn Wharton / NYT / Military Times
timeline_data_point: ~$20B cumulative — $50B supplemental request pending

event_description: Gas estimated at ~$3.69/gallon — weekend data: Based on interpolation from AAA's confirmed $3.675 on March 14 and the ongoing upward trend, national average gasoline is estimated at approximately $3.69 per gallon. The increase of ~$0.71 from pre-war represents a 23.8% jump in just over two weeks. GasBuddy called the week of March 2-9 the 'largest weekly gas price jump of the century.'
timeline_data_point: ~$3.69/gal estimated — +$0.71 / +23.8% from pre-war

event_description: Lebanese casualties reach 826+ killed — IDF ground operations continue: Lebanese Health Ministry reports cumulative death toll from Israeli strikes and ground operations has reached 826+ killed and 2,009+ wounded since March 2. At least 102 children among the dead. 850,000+ displaced. IDF ground operations in southern Lebanon continue with heavy fighting around Bint Jbeil and the eastern sector. Hezbollah's Radwan Force continues to employ IEDs, tunnel systems, and anti-tank missiles against advancing IDF forces.
timeline_source: Lebanese Health Ministry / OCHA / ACLED / CIR
timeline_data_point: 826+ killed — 2,009+ wounded — 850,000+ displaced

event_description: 7 ballistic missile salvos target Israel — cluster munitions in central Israel: Iran launches 7 ballistic missile salvos since midnight targeting central Israel and Eilat. In the Holon area, a cluster bomb warhead injures three people: two men in their 50s lightly injured, one man in his 60s moderately hurt by glass and submunitions. Apartment damage and vehicle fires reported. A separate salvo at Eilat causes no injuries. Iran continues averaging ~10 attack waves per day despite 80%+ degradation of offensive capability.
timeline_source: IDF / Magen David Adom / Israeli media
timeline_data_point: 7 salvos — 3 injured in Holon by cluster munitions

event_description: Australia and Japan refuse to send warships to Hormuz: Despite President Trump's public call for allied nations to deploy warships to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, both Australia and Japan decline. Australia's Defense Minister says the commitment is 'not appropriate at this time.' Japan's PM rules out sending naval assets. No allied nation has made a firm commitment to join US escort operations, underscoring the diplomatic isolation of the US-led war effort.
timeline_data_point: Australia and Japan refuse Hormuz warship request

event_description: Iran FM: 'We never asked for a ceasefire' — diplomacy stalls: Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi declares 'We never asked for a ceasefire' and vows Iran 'is ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes.' The Trump administration separately rebuffs ceasefire efforts by Middle Eastern allies. Oman's mediation attempt fails to produce talks. The Pope calls for an immediate ceasefire. China condemns the attacks. A US Senate vote to curb Trump's war powers fails 47-53.
timeline_data_point: Both sides reject ceasefire — Senate AUMF vote fails 47-53

event_description: 56% of Americans oppose the war — NPR/PBS/Marist poll: A new NPR/PBS NewsHour/Marist poll finds 56% of Americans are against the war with Iran. The figure represents a significant public opinion shift given that the conflict is only 17 days old. Combined with the failed Senate AUMF vote (47-53) and the approaching $50B supplemental request, domestic political pressure on the war effort is building.
timeline_data_point: 56% opposed — first majority-oppose poll of the war

event_description: Brent surges to $106.18 — markets react to Fujairah/Dubai attacks: Brent crude surges 3% to $106.18 per barrel on Monday as markets react to the second Fujairah attack and Dubai airport strike. The rise demonstrates that Iran's ability to target alternative oil routes keeps upward pressure on prices even as its main military capability degrades. S&P 500 futures trade up ~0.5% pre-market near 6,664. Oil options markets price 30% probability of Brent above $120 within two weeks.
timeline_data_point: Brent $106.18 (+3%) — S&P futures +0.5%

event_description: Second Fujairah drone attack — oil loading suspended again: Iran launches a second drone attack on the Fujairah oil hub in the UAE, just two days after the first strike caused fires on March 14. Oil-loading operations are suspended for the second time in three days. Fujairah is the key bypass route for oil exports around the Strait of Hormuz, making these attacks strategically significant in maintaining the effectiveness of the Hormuz closure.
timeline_data_point: Fujairah oil loading suspended twice in 3 days

event_description: Drone strikes Dubai International Airport — fuel tank hit, flights suspended: An Iranian drone strikes a fuel tank near Dubai International Airport, the world's busiest for international passengers. All flights are suspended for several hours. Emirates diverts flights to Abu Dhabi and Al Maktoum Airport; one Dublin-bound flight turns around over Egypt. Emirates resumes limited service after 10am local time. The attack on a major civilian hub represents significant escalation.
timeline_data_point: Dubai airport flights suspended — fuel tank struck by drone

event_description: 1 killed in Abu Dhabi missile strike — Kuwait airport radar damaged: A missile strikes a vehicle in Abu Dhabi's Al Bahyah area, killing one person — the first confirmed fatality in Abu Dhabi from the conflict. Separately, Kuwait International Airport sustains radar equipment damage from an Iranian strike. Both attacks demonstrate continued Iranian targeting of Gulf civilian infrastructure despite 80%+ degradation of offensive capability.
timeline_data_point: 1 killed in Abu Dhabi; Kuwait airport radar damaged

event_description: IDF 91st Division expands ground operations — Khiam targeted: The IDF announces the 91st 'Galilee' Division is pushing deeper into southern Lebanon to expand the buffer zone against Hezbollah. Intense fighting around the strategic town of Khiam, which sits on high ground overlooking northern Israel. At least 3 airstrikes hit Khiam. Israel planning to seize entire area south of Litani River. Defense Minister Katz compares operation to Gaza ground ops. Lebanon indicates readiness for direct ceasefire talks without preconditions.
timeline_source: Al Jazeera / Times of Israel / Axios / CNN
timeline_data_point: 91st Division at Khiam — planning full Litani buffer zone

event_description: Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly wounded — Hegseth: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth states that Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has been wounded, though details are not provided. If confirmed, this would be the second consecutive Iranian Supreme Leader to be directly affected by US-Israeli strikes — his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in the opening hours of the war on February 28.
timeline_data_point: New Supreme Leader reportedly wounded

event_description: NCTC Director Joe Kent resigns — says war started 'due to Israeli lobby pressure': National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent resigned, stating the war was started 'due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby.' Trump called it a 'good thing.'
timeline_data_point: First senior resignation citing Israeli influence

event_description: Trump renounces allied help — 'WE DO NOT NEED THE HELP OF ANYONE': Trump posted on Truth Social rebuking Japan, Australia, South Korea: 'WE DO NOT NEED THE HELP OF ANYONE.' Suggested leaving NATO. Every named ally has declined to send warships for Hormuz escort.
timeline_data_point: All allies declined — Trump threatens NATO departure

event_description: UK shares Hormuz coalition plan with allies: UK drafted a Hormuz escort coalition plan and shared it with the US and allies. However, UK has not committed its own forces. Plan shared via diplomatic channels.
timeline_data_point: UK plan drafted but no forces committed

event_description: Brent pulls back to $101 — S&P recovers to 6,735: Brent crude retreats 5% from Monday's $106.18 peak to $101.00, likely on profit-taking. Oil remains 42.3% above pre-war baseline of $71.00. S&P 500 recovers to 6,734.87, up 1.5% from its 2026 closing low of 6,632. Defense stocks ease slightly from highs. Oil majors pull back with crude. Airlines show modest recovery. Fundamentals unchanged: Hormuz closed, Fujairah intermittently attacked, 12.35 mbd shortfall persists.

War cost estimated at ~$21.7B — $50B supplemental not yet submitted: Cumulative estimated direct military cost reaches approximately $21.7 billion through Day 18, extrapolated from Pentagon's $11.3B through Day 6 and CSIS's $16.5B through Day 12 at ~$850M daily run rate. The $50B supplemental funding request led by Deputy Defense Secretary Steve Feinberg has not yet been formally submitted to Congress. Penn Wharton projects $40-95B total direct costs if conflict reaches two months, with additional $50-210B in economic losses.
timeline_data_point: ~$21.7B cumulative — ~$850M/day — $50B supplemental pending

event_description: Amnesty confirms US responsible for Minab school strike — 168-170 girls killed: Amnesty International published investigation confirming US responsibility for the Minab school strike on Day 1 (February 28). Revised death toll to 168-170 schoolgirls killed. Pentagon investigation still underway months later.
timeline_data_point: 168-170 schoolgirls confirmed killed — US confirmed responsible

event_description: USS Ford fire contained — carrier fully operational after 30+ hour blaze: The non-combat fire aboard USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) that broke out March 12 in main laundry spaces has been contained after burning for more than 30 hours, per New York Times reporting. Two sailors sustained non-life-threatening injuries. Propulsion plant undamaged. Carrier remains fully operational and continues round-the-clock strike operations from the Central Red Sea. Ford has been at sea 265+ days, approaching an 11-month deployment record.
timeline_data_point: Ford fire contained — 265+ days at sea approaching deployment record

event_description: CRITICAL CORRECTION: Ford in Red Sea, not Eastern Med — naval disposition updated: USNI Fleet Tracker (March 16) and Defence Security Asia satellite analysis confirm USS Gerald R. Ford transited the Suez Canal on March 5 and is now operating in the Central Red Sea — NOT the Eastern Mediterranean as previously reported. USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) is deploying from the Atlantic to backfill Ford's vacated Med position. USS America (LHA-6) / 11th MEU has NO confirmed theater presence — actual amphibious asset is USS Tripoli (LHA-7) / 31st MEU, currently transiting South China Sea. Lincoln (CSG-3) repositioned to ~1,100km from Iran with 8 destroyers.
timeline_source: USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16 / Defence Security Asia 3/15 / USNI News
timeline_data_point: Ford in Red Sea — Bush deploying to Med — Tripoli replaces America

event_description: Three LCS mine countermeasure ships operating from Bahrain: Three Littoral Combat Ships — USS Canberra (LCS-30), USS Tulsa (LCS-16), and USS Santa Barbara (LCS-32) — confirmed operating Navy's first operational mine countermeasure mission packages in U.S. 5th Fleet out of Bahrain. Critical asset given confirmed Iranian mine-laying operations in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. has destroyed 30+ Iranian minelayers but Iran deployed 'a few dozen' mines from a stockpile of 2,000-6,000.
timeline_data_point: 3 LCS MCM ships — 30+ minelayers destroyed — dozens of mines laid

event_description: USS Tripoli ARG transits South China Sea — ETA Hormuz March 23-28: USS Tripoli (LHA-7) with 31st MEU (~2,500 Marines, BLT 3/1) continues transit through South China Sea en route to Middle East. Ship spotted sailing south of Taiwan through Luzon Strait on March 13. Naval News estimates 10-15 days to reach Strait of Hormuz area, placing ETA at March 23-28. The ARG includes CG-62 Robert Smalls, DDG-115 Rafael Peralta, and LPD-18 New Orleans. MEU brings F-35B strike capability and potential for island seizure operations near Hormuz.
timeline_source: USNI Fleet Tracker / Naval News / Stars and Stripes
timeline_data_point: ETA March 23-28 — 2,500 Marines + F-35Bs for Hormuz ops

event_description: Strait of Hormuz still closed — zero allied warships committed: Day 18 and the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to Western-allied shipping. Maritime traffic down 97% from pre-war levels. ~1,000 ships anchored near strait entrance including ~200 oil tankers. ~3 shadow fleet/Chinese-flagged vessels transiting daily. Iran continues selective passage for non-Western vessels. No allied nation has committed warships to an escort coalition despite direct Trump pressure on Australia, Japan, UK, France, and Italy.
timeline_data_point: 97% traffic reduction — 0 allied warships committed — ~3 transits/day

event_description: Gulf infrastructure attacks — Fujairah (4th), Abu Dhabi, Umm Al Quwain, tanker struck: Iran continued attacking Gulf targets: Drone hit Fujairah Oil Industry Zone (4th attack since Mar 9). Drone struck Abu Dhabi Shah oil field. Missile killed Palestinian civilian in Al Bahyan, Abu Dhabi. Drone attacked Umm Al Quwain residential building. Kuwait-flagged LPG tanker Gas Al Ahmadiah struck 23nm east of Fujairah (17th vessel attack). UAE total: 314 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,672 drones since Feb 28. 11+ Gulf state civilian deaths (268+ injuries).
timeline_data_point: 17th vessel attack — 314 ballistic + 15 cruise + 1,672 drones at UAE

event_description: USS Tripoli transits Singapore Strait — ~1 week from theater: Reuters footage confirmed USS Tripoli (LHA-7) passing through Singapore Strait. Carrying 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines) with F-35B Lightning IIs (VMFA-121), MV-22B Ospreys, attack helicopters. Escorted by USS Robert Smalls (CG-62) and USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115). ETA operations area approximately one week. MEU capable of amphibious assault and maritime platform seizure.
timeline_data_point: Singapore Strait transit — 2,200 Marines — ETA ~1 week

event_description: Iran retaliatory barrage kills 2 Israeli civilians in Ramat Gan: IRGC launched '57th wave of Operation True Promise 4' — combined drone/missile attacks on Israel and Gulf states. Two Israeli civilians (couple in their 70s) killed in Ramat Gan by cluster-munition warheads from a ballistic missile. 25-year-old wounded by shrapnel in Bnei Brak. Tel Aviv train station damaged. Cumulative Israeli casualties: 16+ killed, 3,400+ wounded. ~50% of ballistic missiles now carry cluster warheads. 290 of 410-440 Iranian launch vehicles destroyed.
timeline_source: NPR / Times of Israel / PressTV / IDF
timeline_data_point: 2 Israeli civilians killed — 16+ total killed — 50% cluster warhead use

event_description: Israel kills Ali Larijani (SNSC Secretary) and Basij chief Soleimani: Israeli airstrikes killed Ali Larijani (Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council) and Gholamreza Soleimani (Basij paramilitary chief) — the third and fourth most senior Iranian officials killed since the war began. Confirmed by Iranian state media. Funeral procession for Larijani drew thousands on March 18.
timeline_source: Al Jazeera / Times of Israel / CNN / CBS News
timeline_data_point: 3rd and 4th most senior officials killed — systematic decapitation

event_description: US destroys Hormuz coastal missile sites with 5,000-lb penetrators: CENTCOM announced US forces employed multiple 5,000-pound deep penetrator munitions against hardened Iranian anti-ship cruise missile sites along the Strait of Hormuz coastline. CENTCOM stated missiles 'posed a risk to international shipping.' No formal press releases since March 14.
timeline_source: CENTCOM via social media / Jerusalem Post
timeline_data_point: 5,000-lb penetrator munitions on hardened Hormuz missile sites

event_description: Israel launches ground invasion of southern Lebanon: IDF launched ground invasion of southern Lebanon, concurrent with Iran campaign. This expands the multi-front war to include a full ground operation beyond the air campaign that began March 2.
timeline_data_point: Ground invasion concurrent with Iran air campaign

event_description: Iran FM Araghchi: 'We need new arrangements for the Strait of Hormuz': Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi told Al Jazeera 'We need to design new arrangements for the Strait of Hormuz,' signaling Iran views the strait as its most powerful remaining leverage and will not relinquish control cheaply.
timeline_data_point: Iran signals Hormuz as primary leverage — no intent to reopen

event_description: Mojtaba Khamenei status unknown — not seen since March 8: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen publicly since his appointment on March 8. Leaked audio suggests minor leg injury. Hegseth claims 'wounded and likely disfigured.' Trump questioned whether he is alive. No new public statement issued. Command authority in Tehran unclear — IRGC may be operating independently.
timeline_data_point: Not seen in 10 days — health unknown — IRGC possibly autonomous

event_description: Brent crude spikes to $108.78 on South Pars news — European gas TTF +7.9%: Brent spiked to $108.78/barrel (+5.6%) on South Pars strike before easing slightly on Iraq-Kurdistan pipeline deal news. WTI traded around $94-96. European gas benchmark TTF jumped 7.9% on South Pars fears. Bitcoin fell below $73,000. Pre-war Brent was ~$67-71/barrel; intraday conflict high was $119.50 on March 10. US gas averages $3.79/gallon (+27%), CA above $5, diesel $5.04 (+34%).
timeline_data_point: Brent $108.78 spike — TTF +7.9% — gas $3.79/gal — diesel $5.04

event_description: IMO emergency session — 3,200 vessels, 20,000 seafarers stranded: IMO convened emergency session in London reporting ~3,200 vessels and ~20,000 seafarers stranded west of the Strait of Hormuz. CBS/60 Minutes: ~700 ships including 400 oil tankers holding 200 million barrels. Lloyd's List: 60 VLCCs (8% of global compliant fleet). Secretary-General Dominguez: 'unacceptable and unsustainable.' Proposed non-binding safe maritime corridor resolution. Insurance premiums at ~5% of vessel value (100x increase).
timeline_source: Euronews / CNBC / IMO / CBS / Lloyd's List
timeline_data_point: 3,200 vessels — 20,000 seafarers — 60 VLCCs — 200M barrels stranded

event_description: Iraq-Kurdistan pipeline restarts at ~250,000 bpd — marginal but symbolic: Iraq resumed oil exports from Kirkuk to Turkey's Ceyhan port at ~250,000 bpd, with potential to reach 450,000 bpd. KRG PM Barzani cited 'extraordinary circumstances.' This represents only 6-13% of Iraq's pre-war southern export volumes (3.4 million bpd from Basra, now halted). Brent briefly eased 1.38% on the news.
timeline_data_point: 250,000 bpd — potential 450,000 — 6-13% of pre-war Basra exports

event_description: War cost reaches ~$22.5B — S&P 500 at 6,677 — energy war premium: Cumulative war cost estimated at ~$22.5 billion through Day 19. S&P 500 at 6,677.00 (-2.9% from pre-war). Defense and oil sectors surge on energy war escalation while airlines decline further. IRGC Gulf energy threats introduce new risk tier for markets.
timeline_data_point: ~$22.5B cumulative — S&P 6,677 — energy war reshapes markets

event_description: Kirkuk-Ceyhan pipeline restarted at 250K bpd — first flow in decade: The Kirkuk-Ceyhan oil pipeline restarted at 250,000 bpd, with potential for 450,000 bpd. First flow in over a decade following Baghdad-KRG agreement. Hydrostatic testing ongoing on 100km section. Represents only 6-13% of Iraq's pre-war Basra exports.
timeline_data_point: Kirkuk-Ceyhan restarted — 250K bpd — first in decade

event_description: Tehran residential strike kills at least 2 — Bushehr nuclear plant hit: Iranian state media reported early Wednesday strikes on northern Tehran neighborhood; emergency crews recovered two bodies. Separately, a projectile struck the Bushehr nuclear power plant — no injuries per IAEA/Rosatom. ~480 Russian citizens remain on site.
timeline_data_point: 2+ killed Tehran — Bushehr nuclear plant struck — 480 Russians on site

event_description: IRGC threatens Gulf energy targets — names SAMREF, Jubail, Al Hosn, Ras Laffan: Following South Pars strike, IRGC issued 'urgent warning' declaring specific Gulf energy facilities as 'direct and legitimate targets in the coming hours': Saudi Arabia's SAMREF Refinery and Jubail Petrochemical Complex, UAE's Al Hosn Gas Field, Qatar's Mesaieed and Ras Laffan facilities. Civilians and workers urged to 'immediately leave.'
timeline_data_point: Named 5 Gulf energy facilities as imminent targets

event_description: US Embassy Baghdad attacked by explosive drones: Explosive drones targeted the US Embassy compound in Baghdad, triggering sirens. Explosion heard near the diplomatic compound.
timeline_data_point: Baghdad embassy compound attacked

event_description: Ford heading to Souda Bay, Crete — MCM ships in Malaysia — MCM crisis: USS Ford transiting to Souda Bay, Crete for 1+ week pierside repairs. ~200 sailors treated for smoke inhalation, ~600 displaced. ~267 days deployed, approaching post-Vietnam record. CRITICAL MCM GAP: 2 of 3 LCS MCM ships (Tulsa, Santa Barbara) spotted in Penang, Malaysia. 4 Avenger-class minesweepers decommissioned Jan 2026. Washington Institute: 16 MCM vessels needed, Navy has 7. Iran retains 80%+ of minelayers.
timeline_source: USNI / Navy Times / Reuters / Washington Institute
timeline_data_point: Ford down for 1+ week — 2/3 MCM ships in Malaysia — 16 needed, 7 available

event_description: CRITICAL: South Pars gas field struck — first upstream energy infrastructure hit: Israeli Air Force, operating with explicit US coordination, struck Iran's South Pars natural gas field infrastructure and the Asaluyeh processing hub in Bushehr province. Phases 3, 4, 5, and 6 taken offline to control fires, halting two refineries with combined capacity of ~100 million cubic meters/day of gas — roughly one-fifth of the field's total capacity. First strike on Iran's upstream energy infrastructure since Operation Epic Fury began, crossing a line Trump had previously drawn. Qatar condemned the strike — South Pars is shared with Qatar's North Dome reserves.
timeline_source: Bloomberg / Axios / Jerusalem Post / Saudi Gazette
timeline_data_point: ~100M cm³/day gas offline — first upstream energy strike — Qatar condemns

event_description: Israel kills Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib — 3rd official in 24 hours: Israeli strike killed Iranian Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib — the fifth most senior Iranian official killed since the war began and the third in just 24 hours (after Larijani and Soleimani on March 17). Confirmed by Iranian state media.
timeline_source: NPR / Al Jazeera / Times of Israel
timeline_data_point: 5th senior official killed — 3 in 24 hours — systematic decapitation

event_description: Riyadh emergency summit — 12 Arab/Islamic FMs — missiles intercepted during meeting: Saudi Arabia hosted emergency consultative meeting of 12 Arab and Islamic foreign ministers (Azerbaijan, Bahrain, Egypt, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Pakistan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Syria, Turkey, UAE). Iranian ballistic missiles intercepted over Riyadh during the meeting itself. Joint statement called on Iran to 'immediately halt its attacks.' Saudi FM: 'almost nothing can re-establish trust.'
timeline_source: Saudi Press Agency / Reuters / Al Jazeera
timeline_data_point: 12 FMs meet — missiles intercepted during summit — Saudi confrontation posture

event_description: Iran FM rejects ceasefire — 'We believe in ending the war on all fronts': Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi told Al Jazeera: 'We don't believe in a ceasefire. We believe in ending the war on all fronts.' He rejected any suggestion Iran was seeking negotiations. Combined with the killing of Larijani — Iran's most prominent pragmatist — this effectively closes the most visible diplomatic channel for de-escalation.
timeline_data_point: Iran explicitly rejects ceasefire — no diplomatic channel visible

event_description: Joe Kent resigns as NCTC Director — highest-ranking Trump official to quit over war: Joe Kent, Director of the National Counterterrorism Center, resigned March 17 — the highest-ranking Trump official to resign over the war. A retired Green Beret with 11 combat tours and Gold Star spouse, Kent posted: 'Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation.' He told Tucker Carlson there was 'no intelligence' of an imminent attack. Now under FBI investigation for allegedly leaking classified information.
timeline_source: NBC News / Tucker Carlson / Joe Kent statement
timeline_data_point: Highest Trump official to resign — under FBI investigation — 'no imminent threat'

event_description: Brent crude spikes to $119.11 — highest of the war — on Gulf energy attacks: Brent crude surged past $118/barrel intraday, reaching $119.11 — the highest since the war began — as Iran's retaliatory strikes on Gulf energy infrastructure hit markets. Session range $109.21-$119.11. WTI approached $100. Diesel trading 'north of $180/barrel.' European gas TTF surged 23-35% to near €67/MWh — highest since early 2023.
timeline_data_point: Brent $119.11 intraday — TTF +35% to €67/MWh — diesel $180+/bbl

event_description: Federal Reserve holds rates at 3.50-3.75% — inflation forecast raised: The Fed held rates at 3.50-3.75% with one dissent (Miran favoring cut). Inflation forecast revised up to 2.7% from 2.5%. Powell: 'Higher energy prices will push up overall inflation, but it is too soon to know the scope and duration.' Seven of 19 FOMC participants projected no rate cuts in 2026. Market rate cut expectations pushed to April 2027.
timeline_data_point: Rates held 3.50-3.75% — inflation raised to 2.7% — cuts pushed to April 2027

event_description: Markets close at 2026 lows — Dow -768 — S&P 6,618 — Nikkei -3.38%: Dow Jones lost 768 points (-1.63%) to 46,225 — new 2026 low below 200-day MA. S&P 500 fell 1.36% to 6,624.70 — lowest closing level of 2026. Nasdaq shed 1.46% to 22,152. VIX at 25.09 (+12%). Global: Nikkei -3.38%, Hang Seng -2.02%, Euro Stoxx 50 -1.71%. Energy stocks outperformed: Occidental +36% Q1; ExxonMobil and Chevron at all-time highs. Brent settled at $100.82.
timeline_data_point: S&P 6,618 new 2026 low — Dow -768 — Brent settled $100.82 — VIX 25.09

event_description: War cost reaches ~$23.4B — gas $3.86/gal — 4.2M+ displaced: Cumulative war cost estimated at ~$23.4 billion through Day 20. Daily cost ~$900M ('largest strike package yet'). National gas average $3.84-$3.88/gal (+29% since pre-war). Diesel near $5.07/gal. Eight states above $4, three above $5. EIA revised 2026 forecast from $2.91 to $3.34/gal. Total displacement: 4.2M+ (Iran 3.2M + Lebanon 1M+). Internet at 4%.
timeline_source: Pentagon / AAA / EIA / CNN / OCHA / NetBlocks
timeline_data_point: ~$23.4B cumulative — $900M/day — gas $3.86 — 4.2M displaced — internet 4%

event_description: Hengaw 5th report: 5,300 killed in 18 days — 511 civilians — 120 children: Hengaw Organization's 5th report documents 5,300 killed in the first 18 days of the war — 511 civilians (9.6%), including 120 minors and 160 women, plus 4,800+ military personnel. Strikes recorded in 178 cities across 25 provinces. Iran Health Ministry separately reports 1,444 killed and 18,551 injured. HRANA documented 3,114 deaths. US: 13 KIA, ~200 wounded.
timeline_source: Hengaw Organization / Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / DoD
timeline_data_point: Hengaw: 5,300 killed — 511 civilian — 120 children — 178 cities — 25 provinces

event_description: Iran strikes Ras Laffan LNG facility with ballistic missile: IRGC strikes Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City — the world's largest LNG production facility handling ~20% of global LNG supply — with at least one ballistic missile, causing fires and 'extensive damage' per QatarEnergy. A second strike followed early March 19. Qatar declared Iranian embassy military attachés persona non grata, invoked Article 51, and condemned the attack as 'a dangerous escalation and a direct threat to national security.'
timeline_data_point: Ras Laffan struck — 20% of global LNG — Qatar invokes Article 51

event_description: Saudi Arabia intercepts 4 ballistic missiles, 12+ drones — SAMREF refinery hit: Riyadh intercepted 4 ballistic missiles and shot down 12+ drones in eastern regions. Debris fell near Prince Sultan Air Base — no damage. Four civilians injured by debris in a Riyadh residential area. A drone struck the SAMREF refinery in Yanbu — the only functioning crude export outlet for Gulf Arab states — described as 'minimal impact.' Saudi FM Prince Faisal bin Farhan said the kingdom 'reserved the right to take military action' against Iran.
timeline_data_point: SAMREF refinery struck — Saudi warns of military action — strongest language yet

event_description: Projectile strikes Bushehr nuclear plant premises — IAEA: 'reddest line of all': A projectile struck Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant premises at ~15:11 GMT March 18, hitting a structure 350 meters from the reactor (metrology service building). IAEA DG Grossi confirmed impact and called it 'the reddest line of all.' Appears to be drone damage. No party claimed responsibility. 250 Rosatom employees partially evacuated. Reactor sustained no damage; radiation levels normal.
timeline_source: IAEA / Rosatom / Fox News / Russia Today
timeline_data_point: 350m from reactor — IAEA 'reddest line' — no party claimed responsibility

event_description: Kuwait refineries hit by Iranian drones — arrests of Hezbollah-linked plotters: Drones targeted units at Mina al-Ahmadi and Mina Abdullah refineries, causing fires at both. Kuwait arrested 10-14 Kuwaiti nationals and 2 Lebanese affiliated with Hezbollah for plotting 'terrorist actions against vital infrastructure.'
timeline_data_point: Two Kuwait refineries struck — 14+ arrested for Hezbollah-linked plots

event_description: UAE suspends Habshan gas operations — Al Minhad Air Base struck near Australian troops: Abu Dhabi's Habshan gas facilities suspended operations after intercepted missile debris fell on site. An Iranian projectile struck near Al Minhad Air Base hosting Australian troops; PM Albanese confirmed 'minor damage' to accommodation and medical facilities, no casualties. Dubai intercepted all inbound threats. Cumulative UAE data: 314 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,672 drones fired by Iran.
timeline_source: Reuters / Australian PM statement / UAE Defense Ministry
timeline_data_point: UAE gas suspended — Australian base struck — 314 BMs + 1,672 drones cumulative

event_description: Hegseth: 7,000+ targets struck — 'largest strike package yet' — 120 vessels destroyed: Defense Secretary Hegseth announced 7,000+ US targets struck across Iran. March 19 would feature 'the largest strike package yet' — approximately 20% larger than previous day. Joint Chiefs Chairman Caine: 120 Iranian vessels and 44 minelayers destroyed. A-10C Thunderbolt IIs hunting fast attack craft in Hormuz. AH-64 Apaches striking Iraqi militias. Pentagon requesting $200+ billion supplemental.
timeline_data_point: 7,000+ US targets — 120 vessels destroyed — $200B supplemental requested

event_description: IDF strikes Caspian Sea for first time — Bandar Anzali naval base: In a major geographic expansion, the IDF struck Iranian Navy vessels at Bandar Anzali on the Caspian Sea coast — approximately 1,300 km from Israel. More than five vessels targeted per Axios. Bandar Anzali hosts Iran's Northern Fleet headquarters and is a key node in the Iran-Russia weapons supply corridor. First Israeli strike in the Caspian Sea region.
timeline_data_point: First Caspian Sea strikes — 1,300km from Israel — Iran-Russia supply corridor

event_description: Hezbollah fires 100+ rockets — first to reach southern Israel — largest barrage yet: Hezbollah fired more than 100 rockets in a single barrage targeting northern, central, and southern Israel simultaneously — the first Hezbollah rocket attack to reach southern Israel since the war began. Synchronized with Iranian ballistic missile salvos. Israel killed 20+ in Lebanon on March 18 including 6 in central Beirut. Israel issued widest forced evacuation in southern Lebanon since 2006 war.
timeline_data_point: 100+ rockets — first to reach southern Israel — widest Lebanon evacuation since 2006

event_description: Iran FM rejects ceasefire — 'We believe in ending the war on all fronts': Iranian FM Araghchi stated March 18: 'We don't believe in a ceasefire. We believe in ending the war on all fronts.' He said there is 'no reason to talk with Americans.' Pre-war Omani mediation had achieved a 'breakthrough' on enrichment — Oman's FM called the US attack a betrayal. Seven-nation joint statement (US, UK, France, Germany, Italy, Japan) expressed readiness for 'preparatory planning' on Hormuz, but France, Germany, Italy, and Japan had all ruled out sending ships. Analysts called it 'largely a gesture to placate Trump.' Trump responded: 'WE DO NOT NEED THE HELP OF ANYONE!' and called NATO allies 'COWARDS.'
timeline_data_point: No ceasefire negotiations exist — no diplomatic track — all allies decline forces

event_description: Nowruz: Mojtaba Khamenei issues written statement — still no video or audio: On Iran's Nowruz new year, Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued a written statement calling the US-Israeli expectation of regime collapse a 'gross miscalculation' and praising national unity. He has still not appeared in video or audio since taking power February 28. President Pezeshkian addressed Gulf neighbors: 'You are our brothers... We have come to resolve all these differences with you.' Trump told Naval Academy midshipmen: 'Their leaders are all gone. The next set of leaders are all gone. And the next set of leaders are mostly gone... We want to talk to them and there's nobody to talk to.'
timeline_source: Iranian state media / White House / Reuters
timeline_data_point: Khamenei still not seen publicly — Iran warns 'zero restraint' if energy hit again

event_description: Trump: 'winding down' — followed 24 hours later by power plant ultimatum: Trump posted on Truth Social: 'We are getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts.' This conciliatory messaging lasted approximately 24 hours before being replaced by the 48-hour ultimatum to destroy Iran's power plants.
timeline_data_point: 'Winding down' rhetoric contradicted by 48-hour ultimatum next day

event_description: Ras Laffan damage assessment: 12.8 MTPA offline, 3-5 year repair, $20B/yr loss: QatarEnergy confirmed LNG Trains 4 and 6 at Ras Laffan were damaged in IRGC strikes March 18-19, representing 12.8 MTPA and 17% of Qatar's LNG exports. Repair timeline: 3-5 years. Force majeure declared on contracts to Belgium, Italy, South Korea, and China. Estimated revenue loss: ~$20 billion per year (~9% of Qatar's GDP). Pearl GTL (Shell-operated) also damaged — offline minimum 1 year. Qatar expelled Iran's military attachés. Wood Mackenzie: 'fundamentally reshaping the global LNG outlook.'
