timeline_data_point: 12.8 MTPA offline — 3-5yr repair — $20B/yr loss — ~9% of Qatar's GDP

event_description: S&P 500 closes at new 2026 low — Quadruple Witching — VIX 25.80: S&P 500 fell to ~6,595 — new 2026 closing low, 4th consecutive weekly loss, and first close below the 200-day moving average in 214 sessions. Dow fell ~148 points to ~45,973. Nasdaq dropped ~0.52%. March 20 was Quadruple Witching Friday, amplifying volatility. VIX surged to 25.80 intraday. Some traders pricing possible 2026 rate hike. Treasury 10-year yield climbed to 6.75-month high of 4.32%. Goldman Sachs warned oil could stay above $100 through 2027.
timeline_data_point: S&P ~6,595 — below 200-day MA — VIX 25.80 — 10yr at 4.32%

event_description: Gas hits $3.91/gallon — Georgia first state to suspend fuel taxes — diesel $5.07: AAA national average on March 20: $3.912/gallon (up from $2.93 on Feb 20 — $0.98/gallon increase, +33% since war began). Diesel: $5.07/gallon (highest since 2022). Georgia became the first state to suspend fuel taxes — a 60-day suspension of 33¢/gal gas tax and 37¢/gal diesel tax, forgoing $360-400M in revenue. California gas averaged $5.34-$5.53/gal. IEA classified the disruption as 'the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market' with at least 8 million bpd curtailed.
timeline_data_point: $3.91/gal (+33%) — diesel $5.07 — GA suspends taxes — IEA: largest disruption ever

event_description: War cost reaches ~$24.3B — $200B supplemental still not sent to Congress: Cumulative estimated cost reaches ~$24.3 billion through Day 21. Pentagon requesting $200B supplemental — Hegseth confirmed request sent to White House but not yet formally to Congress. Trump has not signed off. Bipartisan skepticism intense: Rep. Boebert 'is a no'; Sen. Schumer said $200B 'means he believes we might be in a war for a very, very long time'; Sen. Murkowski demands a plan before funding. No Iran-specific AUMF passed — administration invokes Article II.
timeline_data_point: ~$24.3B cumulative — $200B request in limbo — no AUMF — bipartisan skepticism

event_description: Iraq declares force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields: Iraq declared force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields. Basra production dropped from 3.3M bpd to 900K bpd. Iraq depends on crude sales for 90%+ of government income. Major blow to global oil supply as Iraq was the second-largest OPEC producer before the conflict.
timeline_data_point: Iraq force majeure — Basra from 3.3M to 900K bpd — 90%+ of government income from oil

event_description: Treasury issues GL U — 30-day sanctions waiver on ~140M barrels of Iranian oil at sea: US Treasury issued General License U — a 30-day sanctions waiver (effective through Apr 19) on approximately 140 million barrels of Iranian oil currently loaded on ships at sea. Allows any buyer except DPRK, Cuba, and Russian-occupied Ukraine. Treasury Secretary Bessent: 'Using the Iranian barrels against Tehran to keep the price down.' Analysts note 140M barrels = ~1.5 days of global consumption; impact 'likely short-lived.'
timeline_data_point: GL U waiver — 140M barrels unsanctioned for 30 days — 'likely short-lived' impact

event_description: Markets: Brent $112.19 (+3.26%), S&P 6,506 new 2026 low, gas $3.93: Friday March 20 close: Brent crude $112.19/bbl (+3.26%), WTI $98.32/bbl (+2.27%). RBOB gasoline futures +5.09% — 3.5-year high. S&P 500 at 6,506.48 (−1.51%), new 2026 low. Dow 45,577.47 (−0.96%). Nasdaq 21,647.61 (−2.01%). 3 consecutive weekly losses. National gas average $3.93/gal (AAA Mar 21). Goldman year-end target 7,600 (adverse: 6,300). JPMorgan cut to 7,200. Sunday Mar 22: no US trading.
timeline_source: CNBC / Fortune / Bloomberg / Barchart / AAA via NBC News
timeline_data_point: Brent $112.19 — S&P 6,506 new 2026 low — gas $3.93 — 3 weekly losses

event_description: Saudi Arabia intercepts missile, shoots down 10+ drones — Yanbu only export outlet: Saudi Arabia intercepted a missile over northern Al Jouf and shot down 10 drones in the east and 1 in the north on March 20. SAMREF refinery at Yanbu (400,000 bpd Aramco-ExxonMobil JV) was struck by drone Mar 19 with 'minimal impact.' A ballistic missile aimed at Yanbu port was intercepted. Critically, Yanbu is now Saudi Arabia's only functional crude export terminal due to Hormuz closure — Saudi ramped volume to ~5.9 million bpd through the East-West pipeline.
timeline_data_point: Yanbu only export terminal — 5.9 mbd through East-West pipeline

event_description: USS Boxer ARG leaves San Diego — 4,400 Marines heading to Middle East: USS Boxer (LHD-4) with 11th MEU left San Diego March 19, accompanied by USS Comstock and USS Portland. Carrying 2,200+ Marines plus 2,000 sailors. Originally scheduled for Indo-Pacific, deployment rerouted and accelerated to Middle East. ETA: approximately 3 weeks (early April). Combined with USS Tripoli ARG (ETA March 24-25), this will bring ~4,400 additional Marines to the theater — the largest Marine deployment since the war began.
timeline_data_point: ~4,400 Marines deploying — largest Marine reinforcement of the war

event_description: MCM capability crisis exposed — Christian Science Monitor investigation: The Christian Science Monitor published a major exposé on the mine countermeasure capability deficit. USS Tulsa (LCS-16) and USS Santa Barbara (LCS-32) are in Singapore as of March 18 — thousands of miles from Bahrain. USS Canberra (LCS-30) location uncertain. These are the only 3 dedicated US surface MCM platforms in the region after 4 Avenger-class minesweepers were decommissioned in January 2026. A prior Navy assessment found 'unreliable unmanned vehicles, critical single-point failures, and sonar that couldn't see.'
timeline_source: Christian Science Monitor / Washington Institute / USNI
timeline_data_point: Only 3 MCM platforms — 2 in Singapore — 4 minesweepers decommissioned Jan 2026

event_description: CENTCOM confirms 7,800+ targets — 4 senior Iranian officials killed overnight: CENTCOM confirmed 7,800+ cumulative US targets struck by March 20, up from 7,000+ the prior day. IDF struck 130+ regime infrastructure targets in 24 hours. Four senior Iranian officials confirmed killed March 19-20: Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesperson, IAF airstrike), General Afshin Nakshabandi (Armed Forces HQ representative in Basij), Esmail Ahmadi (IRGC head of intelligence), and Mehdi Karishi (IRGC Aerospace Forces commander, Isfahan sector). Also confirmed: Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 Israeli strike; Hassan Ali Marwan (Hezbollah Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19.
timeline_data_point: 7,800+ US targets — 9+ senior officials killed total — ballistic missile launch rate down ~90%

event_description: UAE intercepts 7 BMs + 15 UAVs — cumulative: 334 BMs, 1,714 drones: UAE air defenses intercepted 7 ballistic missiles and 15 UAVs on March 20. Cumulative totals since Feb 28: 334 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,714 UAVs launched at the UAE. Total UAE fatalities: 8 (2 armed forces, 6 foreign nationals). Abu Dhabi shut Habshan gas facilities after intercept debris damage. UAE dismantled Iran/Hezbollah terrorist network using business fronts.
timeline_data_point: 334 BMs + 15 CMs + 1,714 UAVs cumulative — 8 UAE fatalities

event_description: F-35 makes emergency landing after possible Iranian anti-aircraft fire: A US F-35 fighter jet made an emergency landing at a Middle East airbase on March 19 after a combat mission over Iran. Sources told CNN the aircraft was struck by what is believed to be Iranian anti-aircraft fire. The pilot is in stable condition. If confirmed, this would be the first US jet struck by Iran in the war. CENTCOM is investigating. The incident remains unconfirmed.
timeline_data_point: First US jet potentially struck — pilot stable — under investigation

event_description: Missile strikes Jerusalem's Old City — 300 cumulative attack waves on Israel: An Iranian missile struck Jerusalem's Old City on March 20 — Israeli police photographed shrapnel damage. Multiple salvos targeted Jerusalem near midnight and early morning hours. Cumulative total since Feb 28: 300 Iranian attack waves on Israel. March 19 alone saw 19 separate attack waves — exceptionally high. Iran used cluster munitions on March 19 killing a Thai agricultural worker at Adanim; 3 women killed in Bayt Awwa, West Bank when fragments struck a hair salon.
timeline_data_point: 300 cumulative attack waves — 19 on March 19 alone — 18 Israeli civilians killed total

event_description: 22 countries condemn Hormuz closure — South Korea joins coalition — sanctions lifted: 22 countries released joint statement condemning Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz. South Korea joined the seven-nation Hormuz coalition (now 8: UK, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, Netherlands, Canada, South Korea) expressing 'readiness to contribute.' Italian PM Meloni: no EU state considering military mission. France, Germany, Italy, Japan all ruled out warships. US Treasury temporarily lifted sanctions on Iranian oil already loaded on ships (Mar 21 - Apr 19), expecting ~140M barrels to global supply. Iran dismissed, saying no surplus crude to sell. Houthis have NOT resumed Red Sea attacks despite solidarity declarations.
timeline_source: Joint statement / Reuters / US Treasury / AP
timeline_data_point: 22 countries condemn — South Korea joins — sanctions lifted on loaded oil — Houthis still on sideline

event_description: Trump: 'You don't do a ceasefire when you're literally obliterating the other side': Trump told reporters March 20: 'You don't do a ceasefire when you're literally obliterating the other side.' Iran's FM Araghchi rejected ceasefire talks, demanding 'complete, comprehensive and lasting end to the war' with reparations and security guarantees. IDF Chief Zamir said military is 'about halfway through' campaign. Israeli Defense Minister Katz: offensive would 'increase significantly' in coming week. Trump's Truth Social 'winding down' rhetoric contradicted by 2,500 additional Marines and pending $200B supplemental. Public opposition: 56% (NPR), 53% (Quinnipiac), 53% disapprove (Data for Progress). Fox News: 51-29% say Trump's handling made US 'less safe.'
timeline_source: Reuters / AP / NPR / Quinnipiac / Fox News
timeline_data_point: No ceasefire path — 56% oppose war — 51% say 'less safe' — 'about halfway through'

event_description: Trump 48-hour ultimatum: fully open Hormuz or power plants destroyed: Trump posted on Truth Social at 7:44 PM ET: 'If Iran doesn't FULLY OPEN, WITHOUT THREAT, the Strait of Hormuz, within 48 HOURS... the United States of America will hit and obliterate their various POWER PLANTS, STARTING WITH THE BIGGEST ONE FIRST!' Deadline: March 23, 7:44 PM ET. Iran's largest power plants: Damavand (2,868 MW), Shahid Salimi (2,215 MW), Shahid Rajai (2,043 MW).
timeline_data_point: 48-hour ultimatum — deadline Mar 23 7:44 PM ET — 'obliterate power plants'

event_description: Iran FM Araghchi offers Japan safe passage through Hormuz — Japan declines: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi offered Japan safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz in a Kyodo News interview. Japan declined the unilateral deal, maintaining coalition solidarity.
timeline_data_point: Japan offered safe passage — declined unilateral deal

event_description: Markets: Brent ~$108, S&P at 6-month low, gas $3.91 — Goldman warns of $147: Brent crude trades ~$107-112 (volatile intraday). S&P 500 closed March 20 at 6,506.48 (-1.51%), below 200-day MA for 2nd consecutive day. RSI at 23.34 (deep oversold). Only 43.9% of stocks above their 200-day MAs. VIX at 26.78. US gas at $3.91/gal — highest since Oct 2022. Goldman Sachs warned Brent 'likely to exceed 2008 all-time high ($147.50)' with worst case additional $42/bbl through 2027. Fed held at 3.50-3.75%; 95% probability of April hold. Defense stocks at all-time highs: LMT backlog record $194B; THAAD production ramping from 96/yr to 400/yr. $200B supplemental faces significant Republican resistance.
timeline_source: CNBC / Goldman Sachs / HSBC / AAA / Federal Reserve
timeline_data_point: Brent ~$108 — S&P 6,506 (6-month low) — gas $3.91 — Goldman: could exceed $147

event_description: War cost reaches ~$25.3B — $200B supplemental faces Republican resistance: Cumulative war costs through Day 22 estimated at approximately $22-24 billion based on Pentagon's $11.3B Day 6, CSIS's $16.5B Day 12, and ongoing ~$1B/day run rate. Extrapolated: ~$25.3B. The $200B supplemental request has NOT been formally submitted to Congress. Reps. Boebert, Roy, Burchett, Ogles, and Burlison expressed opposition. GOP leaders 'do not believe they have the votes.' Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct costs, up to $210B total economic impact. Hegseth: 'It takes money to kill bad guys.' Axios: Washington considering plans to blockade or occupy Iran's Kharg Island oil hub.
timeline_source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton / Axios / Congress
timeline_data_point: ~$25.3B cumulative — $200B supplemental faces GOP resistance — Kharg Island plan considered

event_description: Sgt. Pennington death announced — 13th US military fatality: The death of Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26, of Glendale, Kentucky was announced on March 21. US total: 13 killed (7 by enemy fire, 6 in KC-135 crash), approximately 200 wounded with vast majority minor and 180+ returned to duty. F-35 damaged on March 19 confirmed as first combat damage to F-35 by enemy fire — struck by suspected Iranian IR/passive sensor-based air defense. Chinese analysts publicly noted implications for stealth aircraft vulnerability.
timeline_data_point: 13 US KIA — ~200 WIA — F-35 first confirmed combat damage

event_description: Iran strikes Dimona and Arad, Israel — 180-200 injured near nuclear center: Iran launched ballistic missiles with conventional warheads at Dimona and Arad in southern Israel, near the Shimon Peres Negev Nuclear Research Center. At least 2 missiles penetrated air defenses. 180-200 injured including a 12-year-old boy and 5-year-old girl in serious condition. 10+ apartment buildings damaged in Arad, 3 in danger of collapse. Mass casualty event declared at Soroka Hospital, Beersheba. IAEA: no abnormal radiation detected. Iran's Ghalibaf: 'If the Israeli regime is unable to intercept missiles in the heavily protected Dimona area, it is operationally a sign of entering a new phase of the battle.'
timeline_source: Times of Israel / Al Jazeera / Haaretz / Jerusalem Post / PBS/AP / CNN
timeline_data_point: Dimona/Arad struck — 180-200 injured — air defenses failed — near nuclear center

event_description: Iran fires IRBMs at Diego Garcia — 2,500 miles from Iranian territory: Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at the joint US-UK military base on Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean, approximately 2,500 miles (4,000 km) from Iranian territory — far exceeding Iran's previously acknowledged 2,000 km missile range. One missile failed in flight; a US warship fired an SM-3 interceptor at the other. Neither struck the base. IDF Chief of Staff Zamir noted the 4,000 km range puts European capitals including Berlin, Paris, and Rome within Iranian reach. Speculation centers on possible use of space launch vehicle.
timeline_data_point: 2,500-mile strike — Iran's longest-range operational attack — European capitals in range

event_description: Dimona struck — interceptors fail — 47 hospitalized including 12-year-old: An Iranian ballistic missile struck Dimona, the city hosting Israel's Shimon Peres Negev Nuclear Research Center. 47 people hospitalized including a 12-year-old boy in serious condition. IDF confirmed interceptors failed to knock down the missile. IRGC called it 'revenge for Natanz.' This was one of nine separate missile salvos from Iran throughout March 21 — Iran's declared '70th wave of attacks.'
timeline_data_point: 47 hospitalized — interceptor failure — IRGC: 'revenge for Natanz'

event_description: Gulf states under continued barrage — UAE 341 BMs, Bahrain 143, Saudi 47 drones: Gulf state defenses engaged multiple waves: UAE intercepted 3 ballistic missiles and 8 UAVs (cumulative: 341 BMs, 15 cruise missiles, 1,748 UAVs since Feb 28). Bahrain intercepted 2 missiles (cumulative: 143 missiles, 242 drones). Saudi Arabia shot down at least 47 drones including 38 within a three-hour window over its eastern oil-rich region. Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery struck by two more waves of Iranian drones, sparking fires.
timeline_source: UAE MoD / Bahrain Defence Force / Saudi MoD / KUNA
timeline_data_point: UAE: 341 BMs cumulative — Saudi: 47 drones in one day — Kuwait refinery hit again

event_description: Hezbollah fires ~10-rocket barrages — IDF deploys additional armored division: Hezbollah fired ~10-rocket barrages at northern Israel around noon, striking homes in Ma'alot-Tarshiha (5 injured) and Metula. Sirens sounded across Safed, the Galilee, and the Golan Heights. IDF conducted 'targeted ground operation' in southern Lebanon with aircraft and tank support, killing multiple fighters with no IDF casualties. An additional armored division finished preparing to deploy. IDF killed over 570 Hezbollah operatives (including 220 Radwan Force) and struck 2,000+ targets since March 2. Reuters revealed IRGC rebuilt Hezbollah into decentralized 'flat' command with smaller 5-rocket cells. 12 medical workers killed in strike on Hezbollah health center. New Beirut evacuation orders for Haret Hreik, Ghobeiry, Hadath.
timeline_data_point: 570+ Hezbollah killed — 2,000+ targets — decentralized command revealed — 12 medics killed

event_description: Diego Garcia attacked by 2 Iranian IRBMs at 4,000 km range — both miss: Iran fired 2 intermediate-range ballistic missiles (described by IDF as 'two-stage ICBM with range of 4,000 km,' possibly improvised Simorgh space launch rocket) at the US-UK military base on Diego Garcia. Both missed — one malfunctioned in flight, one intercepted. First attack on Diego Garcia in history. Demonstrates Iranian missile range of ~4,000 km — double the previously self-imposed 2,000 km limit. IDF Chief Zamir: 'Europe is within range.' UK approved US use of Diego Garcia and RAF Fairford for 'specific and limited defensive operations' against Iranian missile sites.
timeline_source: Bloomberg / CNN / Times of Israel / Euronews / CNBC / Fox News / WSJ
timeline_data_point: First attack on Diego Garcia — 4,000 km range — Europe within reach — both missiles missed

event_description: Houthis warn of response to Hormuz escalation: Houthis warned they would respond to any escalation including efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Despite repeated threats, Houthis have NOT resumed Red Sea attacks — now Day 23 of 'strategic pause.' Declared 'Hour Zero' Mar 14 but no follow-through. Analysts baffled; possible deal with US/Saudis.
timeline_data_point: Houthis warn but still on sideline — 23 days without Red Sea ops

event_description: Natanz nuclear facility struck — IAEA confirms no radiation increase: The Natanz nuclear enrichment facility — Iran's primary uranium enrichment site — was struck on March 21. Iran's Mizan News confirmed the attack but claimed no radiation leakage. IAEA DG Rafael Grossi reported no increase in off-site radiation and called for 'military restraint to avoid any risk of a nuclear accident.' Israel denied responsibility; Iran blamed a joint US-Israeli strike. Russia condemned as posing 'real risk of catastrophic disaster.' This is the second Natanz strike during Operation Epic Fury and the third since June 2025 Operation Midnight Hammer.
timeline_source: IAEA / Mizan News / Reuters / Russia MFA
timeline_data_point: 2nd Natanz strike in war — IAEA: no radiation increase — Russia condemns

event_description: CENTCOM: 8,000+ targets struck, 130 vessels destroyed — 'largest navy elimination since WWII': Admiral Brad Cooper's Day 22 video update confirmed over 8,000 military targets struck in Iran since February 28, up from 7,800+ on Day 21. The US has flown 8,000+ combat sorties maintaining total air superiority. Cooper described destruction of 130 Iranian naval vessels as 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' 5,000-pound bunker-buster bombs employed against hardened underground facilities along southern coastline. Iranian missile and drone attack rates down 90% from opening days. 50,000+ US service members in CENTCOM AOR.
timeline_data_point: 8,000+ targets — 8,000+ sorties — 130 vessels — 50,000+ troops

event_description: Cluster munitions hit Israeli daycare — 7 impact sites across central Israel: Iranian cluster bombs hit a daycare/kindergarten in Rishon Lezion area of central Israel. The building was empty — no casualties from that specific impact — but 7 impact sites were recorded across the area including damage to residential buildings in Bnei Brak, Shoham, and Rosh Ha'ayin. Separately, missile fragments fell in occupied East Jerusalem, 350 meters from the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound. IDF detected a ballistic missile attack targeting Eilat in southern Israel.
timeline_data_point: 9 salvos at Israel — daycare hit (empty) — 350m from Al-Aqsa

event_description: US strikes Natanz nuclear facility with bunker-buster bombs: US struck the Shahid Ahmadi-Roshan Enrichment Complex at Natanz with bunker-buster bombs (likely GBU-57 MOPs and/or GBU-72 5,000-lb penetrators). Israel denied involvement: 'did not conduct any strikes in the area.' IAEA confirmed no increase in off-site radiation levels. DG Rafael Grossi reiterated call for 'military restraint to avoid any risk of a nuclear accident.' Russia condemned as 'a blatant violation of international law.' Entrance buildings of underground Fuel Enrichment Plant damaged. Third strike on Natanz (after June 2025 and early March 2026).
timeline_source: Times of Israel / Al Jazeera / IAEA / Common Dreams / Euronews / NBC News
timeline_data_point: Natanz struck — IAEA: no radiation — Russia condemns — 3rd strike since June 2025

event_description: Iran responds to ultimatum — threatens destruction of all regional energy infrastructure: Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf threatened on X: 'Immediately after power plants and infrastructure in our country are targeted, vital infrastructure as well as energy and oil infrastructure across the entire region will be considered legitimate targets and will be irreversibly destroyed.' IRGC Khatam Al-Anbiya command warned all US energy, IT, and desalination infrastructure in region would be targeted. Iran also threatened permanent Hormuz closure.
timeline_source: Ghalibaf on X / IRGC / Al Jazeera / Reuters
timeline_data_point: Iran threatens regional energy destruction if power plants hit — permanent Hormuz closure

event_description: USS Tripoli ARG expected to arrive CENTCOM AOR: USS Tripoli (LHA-7) Amphibious Ready Group with 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines) expected to arrive in CENTCOM area of operations. ETA window Mar 22-23. One source (GlobalSecurity.org) references Red Sea/northern Arabian Sea operations but USNI and CENTCOM have not confirmed arrival. Most significant force addition since war began — opens ground/amphibious options.
timeline_data_point: Tripoli arrival imminent — 2,200 Marines — F-35Bs — amphibious capability

event_description: CENTCOM Adm. Cooper video update — 8,000+ targets, 130 vessels, Iran declining: CENTCOM Admiral Brad Cooper released video update: 8,000+ targets struck, 130 Iranian naval vessels destroyed, 8,000+ combat flights. 'Iran's combat capability is on the steady decline, as our offensive strikes ramp up.' Fighter jets used GBU-72 5,000-lb bunker-buster bombs to destroy underground coastal facility storing anti-ship cruise missiles, mobile launchers, plus 'intelligence support sites and missile radar relays.'
timeline_source: Al Jazeera / Stars and Stripes / The Hill (citing CENTCOM Adm. Cooper)
timeline_data_point: 8,000+ targets — 130 vessels — 8,000+ flights — Iran 'on steady decline'

event_description: IDF announces 'wave of extensive strikes' on Hezbollah in Lebanon: IDF announced a new 'wave of extensive strikes' on Hezbollah in southern Lebanon and Dahiyeh (Beirut). Lebanese death toll at 1,001+ including 67 women and 111 children. 1M+ displaced (19% of population). 280+ Hezbollah attacks claimed with ~100 rockets/day into Israel.
timeline_data_point: IDF escalates Lebanon strikes — 1,001+ killed — 1M displaced — ~100 rockets/day

event_description: Trump postpones power plant strikes for 5 days — claims 'productive conversations' with Iran: Trump posted on Truth Social in ALL CAPS that he had 'INSTRUCTED THE DEPARTMENT OF WAR TO POSTPONE ANY AND ALL MILITARY STRIKES AGAINST IRANIAN POWER PLANTS AND ENERGY INFRASTRUCTURE FOR A FIVE DAY PERIOD.' Claimed '15 points of agreement' with Iran. Said Kushner and Witkoff held talks Sunday evening with 'a top person' in Iran. New deadline approximately March 28.
timeline_source: Truth Social / CNBC / Fox Business / Reuters
timeline_data_point: 5-day power plant pause — new deadline ~Mar 28 — '15 points of agreement' claimed

event_description: Iran categorically denies any dialogue with Washington: Iran's Foreign Ministry stated: 'There is no dialogue between Tehran and Washington.' IRIB: 'U.S. president backs down following Iran's firm warning.' IRGC-affiliated Tasnim: 'No negotiations have taken place.' Iran characterized postponement as Trump attempting to reduce energy prices and buy time. The actual channel: indirect messages passed via Oman, Turkey, Egypt, and Pakistan, with Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf as key interlocutor.
timeline_source: Iran FM / IRIB / Tasnim / Fars / Axios / JPost
timeline_data_point: Iran denies talks — intermediaries: Oman, Turkey, Egypt, Pakistan — Ghalibaf key interlocutor

event_description: Markets whipsaw: Brent crashes 13.6% then recovers; Dow surges 960 pts: Brent crude crashed from $112.19 to intraday low of $96.91 (-13.6%) on Trump's postponement, then recovered to settle ~$104. WTI crashed to $85.66. S&P 500 surged ~1.7-2.2% to ~6,628. Dow +960 pts. Gold crashed 6.3% to $4,285.70. Asian markets crashed overnight (Nikkei -3.5%, Kospi -6.5%). Defense stocks fell on de-escalation hopes (LMT -1.58%, RTX -1.28%).
timeline_data_point: Brent -13.6% intraday then recovers — S&P +1.7% — Dow +960 — gold -6.3% — largest oil swing of war

event_description: IEA: worst supply disruption since combined 1973 and 1979 oil shocks: IEA Executive Director Fatih Birol called the crisis 'very severe — worse than the combined 1973 and 1979 oil shocks.' At least 44 energy assets severely damaged across 9 countries. IEA released record 400M barrels from strategic stockpiles. Combined Gulf production cuts exceed 10M bpd. Birol: 'The single most important solution is opening up the Hormuz trade.'
timeline_data_point: IEA: 44+ energy assets damaged, 9 countries — worst since 1973+1979 — 400M bbl released

event_description: AAA: gas hits $3.96 — 23rd consecutive daily increase — highest since Aug 2022: National average gas price hit $3.96/gal — the 23rd consecutive daily increase and highest since August 2022. Up ~$1.00/gal from a month ago. California at $5.53+/gal. Wholesale gasoline futures fell 8.5-9% on Monday's oil plunge, but retail prices lag wholesale by several days. Goldman Sachs raised Brent avg forecast to $110 for March-April.
timeline_data_point: Gas $3.96 — 23rd daily increase — CA $5.53+ — wholesale futures down 9% but retail lags

event_description: USS Ford arrives Souda Bay, Crete for emergency repairs — Lincoln sole carrier: USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) arrived at Naval Support Activity Souda Bay, Crete for emergency pierside repairs following non-combat fire Mar 12. Two sailors injured, 200+ treated for smoke inhalation, 600+ lost berthing. Expected stay at least 1 week. Deployed since June 24, 2025 (~9 months). USS Abraham Lincoln remains sole operational carrier in direct theater.
timeline_data_point: Ford at Souda Bay — Lincoln sole carrier — Tripoli ETA Mar 25-30

event_description: Saudi Arabia expels Iranian military attaché — Gulf states intercept fresh barrages: Saudi Arabia intercepted 3 ballistic missiles aimed at Riyadh plus multiple drones, then expelled Iran's military attaché and 4 embassy staff within 24 hours. Bahrain intercepted 32 drones and 2 ballistic missiles. Kuwait intercepted 9 ballistic missiles in 24 hours. UAE intercepted 8+ UAVs and 3+ ballistic missiles.
timeline_source: Saudi MoD / Bahrain DF / Kuwaiti MoD / UAE MoD / Reuters
timeline_data_point: Saudi expels attaché — Bahrain: 32 drones + 2 missiles — Kuwait: 9 missiles — bombardment continues

event_description: HRW: IDF operations in Lebanon constitute war crimes — 3 Litani bridges destroyed: Human Rights Watch issued report stating IDF operations in Lebanon constitute war crimes: forcible displacement, wanton destruction, and attacks targeting civilians. 1,029+ killed, 1M+ displaced, 31 healthcare workers killed. IDF destroyed 3 Litani River bridges, severing southern Lebanon. Defense Minister Katz ordered demolition of border villages. Finance Minister Smotrich called for Litani as new border.
timeline_data_point: HRW: war crimes in Lebanon — 1,029+ killed — 3 Litani bridges destroyed — potential ground invasion

event_description: IDF launches 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran: The IDF launched a 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran early Monday, targeting 'terror regime targets' in 'the heart of Tehran.' Iranian state media reported airstrikes across multiple areas of the capital. Strikes continued despite Trump's diplomatic signals.
timeline_source: IDF / Iranian state media / Al Jazeera
timeline_data_point: IDF strikes Tehran despite diplomatic pause — 'broad wave'

event_description: Iranian missiles strike over Netanya, Israel — strikes continue both sides: A fresh Iranian missile barrage struck over the Israeli coastal city of Netanya on Monday. Rocket trails photographed. Iranian strikes continued throughout the day. Separately, Hezbollah launched missiles at northern Israel — one person moderately-to-severely wounded in Kiryat Shmona. IDF struck a Quds Force affiliate in Beirut.
timeline_data_point: Netanya struck — Kiryat Shmona casualty — war continues despite diplomatic signals

event_description: Lebanon declares Iran ambassador persona non grata — deadline March 29: Lebanon declared Iran's ambassador persona non grata with a deadline of March 29, but stopped short of fully severing diplomatic relations.
timeline_data_point: Iran ambassador expelled — deadline Mar 29 — not full break

event_description: Iran FM Araghchi: 'We don't ask for ceasefire, but this war must end': Iran FM Araghchi stated 'We don't ask for ceasefire' but said 'war must end' so enemies 'never repeat' such actions. Pakistan FM offered to host talks 'if both sides agree.' Egypt working on 30-60 day ceasefire framework.
timeline_data_point: Iran rejects ceasefire framing — Pakistan offers to host — Egypt 30-60 day framework

event_description: UK PM Starmer convenes emergency meeting on war's economic fallout: UK Prime Minister Starmer convened an emergency meeting on the economic impact of the conflict. UK announced plans to send short-range air defense systems to Gulf allies.
timeline_data_point: UK emergency meeting — short-range air defense to Gulf

event_description: Oil rebounds: Brent ~$103, WTI ~$91 — Iran denies all negotiations: Oil prices rebounded 3-4% as Iran denied Trump's peace talk claims, erasing Monday's crash. Brent rose to ~$103-104 from Monday's settlement of ~$104 (which had recovered from $96.91 intraday low). WTI to ~$91-92. Markets oscillated throughout the day.
timeline_data_point: Brent ~$103-104 — WTI ~$91-92 — +3-4% rebound on Iran denial

event_description: AAA: gas at $3.956 national average — Bahrain UNSC resolution — France alternative: AAA gas price update: $3.956 national average. Bahrain circulated a new UNSC Chapter VII draft resolution on Hormuz authorizing 'all necessary means' for safe passage. France proposed alternative draft without naming Iran and not invoking Chapter VII. Russia/China likely to veto Bahrain's version.
timeline_data_point: $3.956/gal — Bahrain Chapter VII draft — France alternative — Russia/China likely veto

event_description: War cost reaches ~$29.3B — Day 25 summary: Cumulative war cost through Day 25 estimated at ~$29.3 billion. Oil rebounded 3-4% to ~$103 Brent / ~$91 WTI. S&P roughly flat. Gas $3.956. Pakistan emerging as lead mediator — Islamabad meeting being arranged between Ghalibaf and Witkoff/Kushner. 5-day power plant pause deadline ~March 28. New SNSC secretary Zolghadr replaces killed Larijani.
timeline_data_point: ~$29.3B cumulative — oil +3-4% — Pakistan mediating — deadline Mar 28

event_description: Kuwait air defense alarms sound 7 times overnight: Kuwait air defense alarms sounded 7 times overnight during Iranian missile and drone attacks across the Gulf region.
timeline_data_point: 7 alarms overnight — broad Gulf barrage continues

event_description: Saudi Arabia intercepts ~20 drones targeting Eastern Province: Saudi Arabia intercepted approximately 20 drones targeting the Eastern Province oil region, maintaining air defense of critical energy infrastructure.
timeline_data_point: ~20 drones intercepted — Eastern Province oil region targeted

event_description: UAE intercepts ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi: UAE air defenses intercepted a ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi. One Indian national suffered minor injuries from debris.
timeline_data_point: Abu Dhabi missile intercepted — 1 minor injury from debris

event_description: CENTCOM updates: 9,000+ targets, 140+ vessels, 9,000+ combat flights: CENTCOM released updated operational statistics: 9,000+ targets struck (up from 8,000+), 140+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed (up from 130+), 9,000+ combat flights. CBS News confirmed 12+ underwater Iranian mines in the Strait (Maham 3 and Maham 7 types).
timeline_data_point: 9,000+ targets — 140+ vessels — 9,000+ flights — 12+ mines confirmed

event_description: WSJ: Tripoli ARG enters CENTCOM AOR Friday (Mar 25): Wall Street Journal confirmed USS Tripoli (LHA-7) Amphibious Ready Group will enter CENTCOM area of operations approximately March 25 (Friday). Several additional days to reach Hormuz area. Carries ~2,200 Marines (31st MEU) and ~20 F-35Bs.
timeline_data_point: Tripoli enters CENTCOM Friday — 2,200 Marines — ~20 F-35Bs

event_description: Iran launches 7 waves of ballistic missiles at Israel — 4 impacts in Tel Aviv: Iran launched seven waves of ballistic missiles at Israel since midnight. At least 4 impact sites confirmed in central Tel Aviv with buildings damaged and vehicles burning. Magen David Adom reported 6 people wounded. Sirens sounded in Dimona near Negev Nuclear Research Center. A cluster munition struck Nesher near Haifa with no injuries. 4 people including 2 infants injured by missile fragment in a southern Bedouin community.
timeline_source: CNN / Magen David Adom / Times of Israel
timeline_data_point: 7 missile waves — 4 Tel Aviv impacts — 6 wounded — Dimona sirens — Nesher cluster munition

event_description: IRGC attacks US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain — 1 Emirati soldier KIA: IRGC launched missiles and drones at US 5th Fleet headquarters in Bahrain. One Emirati soldier killed alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in a direct Iranian attack on Bahrain. Bahrain cumulative: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted since war began.
timeline_source: Al Jazeera / Bahrain MoD / Times of Israel
timeline_data_point: 1 Emirati KIA — first UAE combat death in Bahrain — 153 missiles, 301 drones cumulative

event_description: Israel DM Katz announces intent to occupy S. Lebanon to Litani River: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz made the first explicit statement of intent to occupy southern Lebanon up to the Litani River. IDF struck Hezbollah command centers in Beirut's southern suburbs and destroyed a bridge linking southern Lebanon to the Bekaa Valley.
timeline_source: Reuters / Al Jazeera / Times of Israel
timeline_data_point: First explicit occupation statement — Litani River as objective

event_description: IDF strikes Hezbollah command centers in Beirut and S. Lebanon bridges: IDF struck Hezbollah command centers in Beirut's southern suburbs after issuing evacuation warnings. Also struck a bridge linking southern Lebanon to the Bekaa Valley and bombed a petrol station in southern Lebanon. Finance Minister Smotrich called for annexing southern Lebanon to the Litani River.
timeline_source: Al Jazeera / Times of Israel / IDF
timeline_data_point: Beirut command centers — S. Lebanon bridge — Smotrich: annex to Litani

event_description: IAF strikes 50+ targets overnight in Iran — IDF cumulative 3,000+: Israeli Air Force struck more than 50 targets overnight in Iran including missile launch sites and military facilities. IDF cumulative total since war began: 3,000+ targets in Iran. Strikes continued despite Trump's 5-day power plant pause.
timeline_data_point: 50+ targets overnight — IDF cumulative 3,000+ Iran targets

event_description: US strike in Iraq's Anbar Province targets Iran-backed commander: US forces struck the headquarters of an Iran-backed group in Iraq's Anbar Province, targeting senior commander Saad Dawai.
timeline_data_point: Anbar Province — Iran-backed commander Saad Dawai targeted

event_description: Fars News: 'Iran will not accept a ceasefire' — equity futures cut gains: Fars News Agency reported ~8:00 AM ET that Iran will not accept a ceasefire. Pre-market equity futures, which had surged >1% on ceasefire-plan headlines, were immediately cut in half.
timeline_data_point: Iran rejects ceasefire — futures gains halved

event_description: Iran rejects US 15-point ceasefire plan as 'extremely maximalist and unreasonable': Iran responded to the US 15-point ceasefire plan via Al Jazeera: 'extremely maximalist and unreasonable — it is not beautiful even on paper.' Iran outlined 5-7 counter-conditions including closure of all US bases in Gulf, reparations, Hormuz transit-fee arrangement, end to Israeli strikes on Hezbollah, lifting all sanctions, and preservation of missile program. IRGC spokesman Zolfaqari: 'People like us will never make a deal with you. Not now. Not ever.'
timeline_source: Al Jazeera / Fars News / Iranian state media
timeline_data_point: 15-point plan rejected — 5-7 counter-conditions — 'never make a deal'

event_description: AP-NORC poll: 59% of Americans say war has 'gone too far': AP-NORC poll published showing 59% of Americans say military action has 'gone too far.' 45% extremely or very concerned about gas affordability. Significant shift in domestic opinion.
timeline_data_point: 59% say 'gone too far' — 45% gas affordability concerns

event_description: Speaker Johnson: 'Operation Epic Fury is almost done': Speaker Mike Johnson told reporters 'Operation Epic Fury is almost done... I think we're wrapping up Operation Epic Fury, and I think it will be done in short order.' Said $200B supplemental would 'probably' be part of reconciliation bill.
timeline_data_point: Speaker: 'almost done' — supplemental in reconciliation bill

event_description: Iran's Hormuz letter to UN/IMO — 'non-hostile' vessels may transit: Iran sent a letter to the UN and IMO stating 'non-hostile vessels' may transit the Strait of Hormuz after coordination. No passage for US or Israeli vessels. Transit fees confirmed at $2M per vessel. Marks shift from total closure to selective Iranian-controlled passage system.
timeline_data_point: Hormuz partial opening — $2M transit fee — no US/Israeli vessels

event_description: Diplomatic channels active — Pakistan, Egypt, Turkey, China: White House arranging possible in-person talks in Pakistan 'this weekend' (2 Trump officials → CNN). US agreed 'in principle.' Egypt FM says Cairo 'ready to host.' Turkey confirmed 'playing a role passing messages.' China 'supports all efforts conducive to easing tensions.'
timeline_data_point: Pakistan in-person talks this weekend — Egypt, Turkey, China active

event_description: Brent crude closes at $96.68 — largest single-day drop of war (−3.5%): Brent crude closed at $96.68, down 3.5% — the largest single-day drop since the war began. Intraday sank ~7% to near $93 on ceasefire-plan headlines before recovering after Iran rejection. S&P +0.6% to ~6,596. Gold rebounded +2.1% to $4,560-4,568. All 11 S&P sectors green except energy producers.
timeline_data_point: Brent $96.68 (−3.5%) — S&P +0.6% — gold +2.1% — all sectors green except energy

event_description: War cost reaches ~$30.5B — Day 26 summary: Cumulative war cost through Day 26 estimated at ~$30.3-30.8 billion. Pentagon sent White House $200B supplemental funding proposal. CSIS estimates replenishment cost at $3.1B+ rising ~$758M/day. Penn Wharton projects $40-95B direct military, $50-210B economic losses. 82nd Airborne deploying. 15-point ceasefire plan rejected. Brent at lowest since early war at $96.68. AP-NORC: 59% say war 'gone too far.'
timeline_source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton / AP-NORC / Multiple
timeline_data_point: ~$30.5B cumulative — $200B supplemental — 59% oppose — Brent $96.68

event_description: Iran targets Israel's Orot Rabin power plant: Iran targeted Israel's Orot Rabin power plant in Hadera — the country's largest energy facility providing 20-25% of electricity. No damage or injuries reported. Missile defense intercepted successfully.
timeline_data_point: Largest Israeli power plant targeted — no damage

event_description: Kuwait International Airport drone strike — fuel tank fire: Drone struck Kuwait International Airport hitting a fuel tank, causing a large fire. No casualties reported. Kuwait stated 'damage limited to property.'
timeline_data_point: Airport fuel tank hit — no casualties

event_description: Saudi Arabia intercepts 32+ drones and 1 ballistic missile in 11 hours: Saudi air defenses intercepted 32+ drones and 1 ballistic missile targeting the Eastern Province near Ras Tanura, Ghawar, and Abqaiq energy facilities over an 11-hour period.
timeline_data_point: 32+ drones, 1 ballistic missile intercepted over Eastern Province

event_description: Bahrain air raid sirens activated — cumulative: 153 missiles, 301 drones intercepted: Bahrain activated air raid sirens during continued Iranian attacks. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted by Bahrain.
timeline_data_point: 153 missiles + 301 drones intercepted since Feb 28

event_description: Pentagon confirms 82nd Airborne deployment to CENTCOM: Pentagon formally confirmed deployment of 82nd Airborne Division elements to CENTCOM: HQ elements, division enablers, and 1st BCT. ~1,000-2,000 troops under Maj. Gen. Brandon Tegtmeier. IRF can deploy within 18 hours. First ground combat unit ordered to theater. Possible mission includes Kharg Island seizure.
timeline_data_point: 82nd Airborne — first ground combat unit to theater — ~2,000 troops

event_description: IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure: IDF launched new wide-scale attacks on Iranian government infrastructure, including targets in Tehran, Qazvin (northwestern Iran), and an Iranian submarine research center in Isfahan. South Tehran strike killed at least 12, wounded 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroyed school and residential buildings.
timeline_data_point: Tehran, Qazvin, Isfahan struck — 12 killed in south Tehran

event_description: IRGC fires precision-guided missiles and drones at Israel and US-hosting bases: IRGC launched precision-guided missiles and drones at Israel and US-hosting bases in Kuwait, Jordan, and Bahrain. Missile hits Tel Aviv street — air defenses fail to intercept. Significant property damage, vehicles destroyed. Missile alerts triggered across Israel including Dimona, Eilat, Netanya, and central Israel.
timeline_data_point: Tel Aviv street hit — Dimona sirens — Eilat, Netanya alerts

event_description: Hezbollah fires 100+ rockets at Israeli positions: Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem vowed to fight 'without limits' and rejected negotiations 'under fire.' Hezbollah fired 100+ rockets at Israeli positions in Naqura, Qawzah, and northern Israel.
timeline_data_point: 100+ rockets — Qassem: fight 'without limits'

event_description: 6 Kurdish Peshmerga killed in Iranian missile strikes near Erbil: 6 Kurdish Peshmerga killed and 30 wounded in Iranian ballistic missile strikes north of Erbil, Iraqi Kurdistan.
timeline_data_point: 6 Peshmerga KIA, 30 wounded — Iranian missiles near Erbil

event_description: Israeli strikes on Beirut southern suburbs after evacuation warnings: IDF struck Beirut's southern suburbs after issuing renewed evacuation warnings. Streets covered in debris per AFP. Israel declared 'security zone' south of Litani River (~30 km). Defense Minister Katz said displaced Lebanese 'will not return south of the Litani.' Claims all bridges over Litani destroyed.
timeline_data_point: Beirut suburbs struck — Israel declares Litani security zone

event_description: Iraq grants PMF 'right to respond' — 7 Iraqi soldiers killed in Habbaniyah: Iraq granted Popular Mobilization Forces 'right to respond' to US strikes after Habbaniyah attack killed 15 PMF fighters (Mar 23). 7 Iraqi soldiers killed, 13 wounded in Habbaniyah Military Clinic strike (Anbar Province). Iraq summoned US and Iranian diplomats. Islamic Resistance in Iraq claimed 23 operations in 24 hours.
timeline_data_point: PMF 'right to respond' — 7 Iraqi soldiers KIA — 23 militia ops in 24 hrs

event_description: Iran formally responds to US 15-point ceasefire plan via intermediaries: Iran formally responded to the US 15-point ceasefire plan overnight through intermediaries, per Tasnim news agency, and stated it is 'awaiting the other side's response.' Iran issued its own 5-point counterproposal: complete halt to aggression, guarantee mechanisms, war reparations, comprehensive ceasefire across all fronts, and recognition of Iran's sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz. A senior Iranian official told Reuters the proposal was 'one-sided and unfair' but 'a path forward might still be found if realism prevails in Washington.'
timeline_data_point: Iran's 5-point counterproposal — 'awaiting Washington's reply'

event_description: Trump posts on Truth Social: 'get serious soon, before it is too late': Trump posted on Truth Social telling Iran to 'get serious soon, before it is too late, because once that happens, there is NO TURNING BACK.' US envoy Steve Witkoff told reporters Iran is 'seeking an off-ramp' and there are signs Tehran is interested in the 15-point plan. AP headlined: 'Iran and the US harden their positions.'
timeline_data_point: Trump: 'NO TURNING BACK' — Witkoff: Iran 'seeking off-ramp'

event_description: Trump cabinet meeting — confirms 4-6 week war, reveals Iran tanker gesture: During a cabinet meeting, Trump confirmed he expects the war to last 4-6 weeks and described Iran's leadership as 'begging to make a deal' while being 'militarily obliterated.' Revealed Iran allowed ~8 tankers through Strait as goodwill gesture. White House press secretary Leavitt: 'President Trump does not bluff and is prepared to unleash hell.'
timeline_data_point: Trump: 4-6 weeks — Iran 'begging' — ~8 tankers allowed through

event_description: Pakistan mediating possible in-person talks March 28: Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif confirmed Pakistan is relaying messages between the US and Iran, pushing for possible in-person talks as early as Friday, March 28, in Pakistan. Israel reportedly removed FM Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf from its target list at Pakistan's request to preserve negotiation channels. Ghalibaf — believed to be Trump's unnamed 'respected' Iranian interlocutor — has repeatedly denied any talks.
timeline_data_point: Pakistan talks possible March 28 — Araghchi/Ghalibaf removed from target list

event_description: Senate blocks war powers resolution 53-47; Republican fractures deepen: Senate blocked another war powers resolution 53-47. Rep. Meeks moves to subpoena Rubio, Witkoff, and Kushner for Iran war hearing. Key Republican fractures: Rep. Rogers (Armed Services chair) complained about lack of answers; Rep. Mace won't support ground troops; Rep. Roy called $200B 'a lot of money.' No AUMF has been passed. The 60-day War Powers clock expires April 29.
timeline_data_point: War powers blocked 53-47 — Meeks subpoena motion — War Powers expires Apr 29

event_description: Brent crude surges past $105 on ceasefire collapse and Tangsiri killing: Brent crude surged to $105.85-$106.12, up 3.8-6.4% from previous session. WTI hit $93.61 (+3.6%). The Brent-WTI spread of $12.45 reflects acute premium for waterborne crude amid Middle East disruption. Goldman Sachs described pricing as 'a geopolitical risk premium over fundamentals.' Gold fell to ~$4,429 (-2.4-2.9%) — unusual decline driven by rising bond yields and dollar strength.

Gas hits $3.98 — up $1.00 in one month — highest since August 2022: AAA reports the national average at $3.981/gallon — up $1.00 in one month from $2.98 on February 26 and the highest since August 2022. California leads at $5.79-$5.82, while Oklahoma is cheapest at $3.24-$3.25. Diesel hit $5.10/gallon. USPS has imposed an 8% fuel surcharge on packages.
timeline_data_point: Gas $3.98 — +$1.00/month — CA $5.82 — diesel $5.10 — USPS 8% surcharge

event_description: UNHCR publishes $454.2M emergency refugee response plan: UNHCR published an emergency Inter-Agency Refugee Response Plan requiring $454.2 million. Total regional displacement: 4.1 million internally displaced, 117,000 seeking refuge in other countries. 24,600 Afghans returned from Iran to Afghanistan. IFRC emergency appeal of $51.5 million launched to support 5 million people over 16 months.
timeline_data_point: $454.2M UNHCR appeal — 4.1M displaced — 117K refugees — 24,600 Afghans returned

event_description: 82nd Airborne en route as Iran fortifies Kharg Island: Approximately 2,000 paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne's Immediate Response Force were ordered to the Middle East, including division commander Maj. Gen. Tegtmeier and two battalions. CNN reported Iran is actively building up defenses on Kharg Island in anticipation of a US ground assault. NPR aired interview discussing the 82nd Airborne's capabilities.
timeline_source: Stars and Stripes / CBS News / CNN / NPR
timeline_data_point: ~2,000 paratroopers — Iran fortifying Kharg Island — arrival not yet confirmed

event_description: Sgt. Benjamin Pennington KIA announced — 13th US service member killed: The Department of War announced the death of Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26, of Glendale, Kentucky, supporting Operation Epic Fury. Total US KIA stands at 13 service members, with 290 wounded (255 returned to duty, 10 seriously wounded).
timeline_data_point: 13 US KIA — 290 WIA (255 RTD, 10 seriously wounded)

event_description: Day 27 summary — power plant deadline 48 hours away: Day 27 marks a pivotal inflection: Tangsiri's elimination coincides with diplomatic deadlock and the approaching March 28 power plant strike deadline. Trump's 5-day pause on energy infrastructure strikes (March 23) expires approximately March 28. Convergence of power plant deadline, 82nd Airborne transit, and Tripoli ARG arrival (~March 27) suggests highest-risk escalation window since war began. War cost ~$31.8B. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market' — 10 mb/d Gulf production shut in.
timeline_data_point: ~$31.8B cumulative — power plant deadline ~Mar 28 — Tangsiri killed — ceasefire deadlocked

event_description: CENTCOM confirms USS Abraham Lincoln continuing flight operations: CENTCOM confirmed via a post on X that USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72) continues flight operations against military targets in Iran while sailing in regional waters — directly rebutting Iranian Navy claims that the carrier had been forced to reposition under cruise missile fire. The US has now struck two-thirds of Iran's missile and drone production facilities. By March 25, CENTCOM had conducted 9,000+ combat sorties striking 9,000+ military targets.
timeline_data_point: 9,000+ targets — 9,000+ sorties — 2/3 missile/drone production destroyed

event_description: Iranian missiles strike multiple Israeli locations: Two salvoes of Iranian missiles targeted central and northern Israel within 30 minutes, with impact sites reported in Kafr Qasim, Ganei Tikva, Kiryat Ata, and Tel Aviv. CENTCOM stated Iranian weapons capability is 'down 90%+' compared to pre-war levels. Two people were killed in Abu Dhabi from intercepted missile debris, and a fire was reported at a facility in Bahrain. Saudi Arabia intercepted a drone in its Eastern Province.
timeline_data_point: Missiles hit 4 Israeli locations — 2 killed in Abu Dhabi — Iran weapons 'down 90%+'

event_description: Hezbollah rocket kills man in Nahariya; IDF soldier killed in Lebanon: A man in his 30s was killed and another in his 50s seriously injured in a Hezbollah rocket strike on Nahariya in northern Israel. An IDF soldier was killed in a gunfight with Hezbollah in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem rejected truce talks, saying negotiations 'under fire amounts to imposing surrender.' Hezbollah/Amal ministers boycotted the Lebanese cabinet session over the expulsion of Iran's ambassador.
timeline_source: Times of Israel / Al Jazeera / IDF
timeline_data_point: 1 killed Nahariya — 1 IDF KIA — Qassem: 'negotiations under fire = surrender'

event_description: Israel kills IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri in Bandar Abbas precision strike: Israel killed IRGC Navy Commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri in an overnight precision strike at a covert naval command center in Bandar Abbas. Based on joint US-Israeli intelligence, the strike also killed senior operational staff including intelligence and operations chiefs. Tangsiri had personally ordered the Strait of Hormuz closure on March 2 and directed the harassment campaign against commercial shipping.
timeline_data_point: IRGC Navy commander killed — architect of Hormuz blockade eliminated

event_description: Israel launches 'wave of extensive strikes' on central Isfahan: Israel simultaneously launched a 'wave of extensive strikes' on central Isfahan, targeting remaining missile and drone production infrastructure. Israeli military sources told NPR they want 'several more weeks of war' and are 'speeding up targeting over the next 48 hours' focusing specifically on arms factories. An Al Jazeera correspondent in Tehran reported strikes 'increasing in number and in intensity.'
timeline_data_point: Isfahan arms factories targeted — IDF: 'speeding up targeting over next 48 hours'

event_description: Israeli Defense Minister Katz calls Tangsiri strike a 'clear message': Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz called the Tangsiri strike 'significant news for American partners' and a direct step toward reopening the Strait of Hormuz.
timeline_data_point: Katz: 'significant news for American partners'

event_description: Trump extends power plant strike deadline 10 days to April 6: Trump posted on Truth Social: 'As per Iranian Government request, please let this statement serve to represent that I am pausing the period of Energy Plant destruction by 10 Days to Monday, April 6, 2026, at 8 P.M., Eastern Time. Talks are ongoing and... they are going very well.' Amnesty International, Human Rights Watch, and UN officials had characterized the original threat as 'an open threat to possibly commit a war crime.' The extension provides diplomatic space for Pakistan-mediated talks.
timeline_data_point: Power plant deadline extended to April 6 — 'talks ongoing and going very well'

event_description: G7 summit fractures — no communiqué produced amid deep US-allied divisions: Secretary of State Marco Rubio attended Day 2 of the G7 foreign ministers summit at Vaux-de-Cernay, France — his first overseas trip since the war began. Bloomberg reported Rubio and counterparts 'traded barbs' over Iran and Ukraine. No final communiqué was produced. Rubio urged allies to 'step up' on reopening Hormuz and warned NATO the US will 'never forget' their refusal to help. EU's Kallas accused Russia of providing intelligence to Iran 'to kill Americans.' Germany's Wadephul said Washington has 'no exit strategy.' France hosted military talks with 35 countries on reopening the strait.
timeline_data_point: No communiqué — Rubio: 'never forget' — Kallas: Russia aiding Iran — France: 35-country military talks

event_description: Pakistan confirms indirect US-Iran talks — in-person meeting possible this weekend in Islamabad: Pakistan's Deputy PM/FM Ishaq Dar publicly confirmed for the first time that 'US-Iran indirect talks are taking place through messages being relayed by Pakistan,' with Turkey and Egypt also supporting. NBC News reported an in-person meeting could occur as soon as this weekend in Islamabad between senior US and Iranian officials. Pakistan reportedly persuaded the US to have Israel remove FM Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Qalibaf from Israel's hit list, arguing 'if they are also eliminated then there is no one else to talk to.' Iran rejected the 15-point plan as 'extremely maximalist and unreasonable' and submitted a 5-point counterproposal.
timeline_source: NBC News / Reuters / Pakistan FM office
timeline_data_point: In-person Islamabad talks possible this weekend — Pakistan confirms relay of messages

event_description: Iran FM Araghchi addresses UN Human Rights Council — accuses coalition of genocide: FM Abbas Araghchi addressed the UN Human Rights Council by video on March 27, accusing the US-Israel coalition of 'clear intent to commit genocide,' claiming 600+ schools damaged and 1,000+ students/teachers killed or wounded. Araghchi stated: 'No negotiations have happened with the enemy until now, and we do not plan on any negotiations.' UNSC held an urgent debate on the Minab school strike (175+ killed, mostly girls aged 7-12), requested by Iran, China, and Cuba.
timeline_data_point: Araghchi: 'clear intent to commit genocide' — UNSC debate on Minab school — 600+ schools damaged

event_description: Brent crude surges past $108 — up 5.66% on Iran rejection and deadline extension: Brent crude surged to $108.01/bbl (+5.66%) and WTI to $94.48 (+4.61%) after Iran's rejection of the 15-point peace framework. Oil held gains despite Trump's deadline extension. Gold rose to ~$4,430-$4,454/oz (+$53, +1.2%). Brent has surged approximately 52% since February 28. US gas remains at $3.98/gallon national average. IEA describes this as the biggest energy supply disruption ever measured by barrels per day.

S&P 500 closes at 6,477 (-1.74%) — VIX jumps to 27.44: The S&P 500 closed at 6,477.16 (-1.74%), the Dow at 45,960.11 (-1.01%), and the Nasdaq at 21,408.08 (-2.38%) on March 26. The VIX surged 8.33% to 27.44. March 27 futures recovered modestly (+0.39%) on the deadline extension and tanker goodwill gesture. Energy stocks up 18.2% MTD, defense up 19-22% MTD. Airlines down 19-24% MTD. Jet fuel costs up ~31% since March 1.

ICRC: 'civilians bearing the brunt' — Philippines declares state of emergency: ICRC President Mirjana Spoljaric Egger stated 'civilians are bearing the brunt' and called attacks on civilian infrastructure 'unacceptable.' Up to 3.2 million Iranians are internally displaced. 830,000 Lebanese displaced (20% of population). 25 hospitals damaged in Iran, 9 out of service. 42,000+ civilian sites damaged. IFRC launched CHF 40 million emergency appeal for Iran. The Philippines declared a state of emergency over the energy crisis with fuel prices more than doubled and nationwide protests in Manila.
timeline_data_point: 3.2M displaced Iran — 830K Lebanon — 25 hospitals damaged — Philippines emergency

event_description: CENTCOM: US has struck over 10,000 Iranian targets — largest navy elimination since WWII: CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper confirmed the US has struck over 10,000 Iranian targets, destroyed two-thirds of Iran's arms manufacturing facilities, flown 8,000+ combat flights, and eliminated 130+ Iranian naval vessels and 44 minelayers. Cooper stated this represents 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' Pentagon assesses Iranian missile and drone attacks are down 90% from the early days of the war.
timeline_data_point: 10,000+ targets — 130+ naval vessels — 44 minelayers — 8,000+ flights — 'largest navy elimination since WWII'

event_description: Iran fires 7 missile salvos at Israel — cluster warheads hit near Netanya and Kfar Qasim: Iran fired at least 7 missile salvos at Israel within hours on March 26-27, many armed with cluster warheads. Missiles triggered sirens in Jerusalem, the West Bank, and central Israel. Strikes hit areas near Netanya, Kfar Qasim, and Ganei Tikva, wounding at least 10 people. IRGC drones struck Haifa port in what Iran described as its 82nd wave of attacks. Kuwait's National Guard shot down two Iranian drones on March 27, and the UAE and Kuwait both reported interceptions. Falling debris from an intercepted missile killed 2 people in Abu Dhabi on March 26.
timeline_source: Times of Israel / CENTCOM / IDF / Reuters
timeline_data_point: 7 salvos at Israel — 10 wounded — Haifa port struck — 2 killed Abu Dhabi — Kuwait intercepts 2 drones

event_description: 82nd Airborne paratroopers deploying — Pentagon considers up to 10,000 troops: The Pentagon confirmed ~2,000-3,000 paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne Division's 1st Brigade Combat Team are deploying to the CENTCOM AOR, led by Maj. Gen. Brandon Tegtmeier. Initial elements were expected to begin arriving 'within a week' of the March 24 announcement. The Wall Street Journal reported on March 26 that the Pentagon is considering up to 10,000 additional ground troops for the theater — the largest ground deployment since the Iraq War. Iran is actively fortifying Kharg Island.
timeline_source: Stars and Stripes / WSJ / CBS News / CNN
timeline_data_point: 2,000-3,000 82nd Airborne + up to 10,000 more — largest ground deployment since Iraq

event_description: Strait of Hormuz: fewer than 6 vessels per day — 2,000 vessels stranded: Pre-war traffic of ~110-138 vessels per day has plummeted to fewer than 6 per day. Only 21 tankers transited between March 1-15 (S&P Global). Approximately 2,000 vessels and 20,000 seafarers are stranded on both sides. Eight 'dark ships' detected transiting with AIS off. IRGC operating a vetting and toll system charging up to $2 million per transit. VLCC freight rates hit all-time high of $423,736/day. Insurance premiums surged from 0.125-0.25% to 5-10% of hull value.
timeline_data_point: <6 vessels/day — $2M transit tolls — $423,736/day VLCC rates — 2,000 vessels stranded

event_description: Day 28 summary — war at inflection point with three converging timelines: Day 28 finds Operation Epic Fury at a critical juncture. Militarily: 10,000+ targets, 92% of navy, 2/3 arms manufacturing destroyed. Diplomatically: Trump extends deadline to April 6; Pakistan pushes for in-person Islamabad talks this weekend; G7 fractures without communiqué. Economically: Brent $108, gas $3.98, airlines hemorrhaging, Philippines in emergency mode. The next 72 hours are pivotal — if Pakistan-mediated face-to-face talks materialize this weekend, they would be the first direct US-Iranian contact of the war. Estimated cumulative cost: ~$33.1B.
timeline_data_point: ~$33.1B cumulative — deadline Apr 6 — Islamabad talks possible — 10,000+ targets — G7 fractures

event_description: IDF launches heaviest overnight strikes deep into Tehran and western Iran: Israeli Air Force conducted what the IDF called a 'wide-scale wave of strikes' in 'the heart of Tehran' overnight, hitting dozens of weapon manufacturing sites including Iran's 'most central' naval arms production facility, a ballistic missile component factory, an anti-aircraft system storage base, and a weapons battery production site. Strikes also hit Kermanshah and Dezful in western Iran with approximately 70 bombs targeting missile launch sites, ballistic missile storage, and air defense systems. IDF Spokesman Brig. Gen. Defrin confirmed Iranian soldiers killed at those sites. IDF has now struck more than 1,000 targets in Iran's weapons production sector alone. Six civilians reported killed in Qom and additional casualties in Urmia from overnight strikes on residential buildings.
timeline_data_point: 1,000+ weapons targets struck — 70 bombs on Kermanshah/Dezful — 6 killed in Qom

event_description: IDF Sgt. Volansky killed in Lebanon — Hezbollah fires 100+ rockets: IDF Sgt. Aviaad Elchanan Volansky, 21, was killed by anti-tank fire in southern Lebanon. A civilian woman was killed in Nahariya by Hezbollah rocket fire, and 14 others were wounded. An IDF officer and soldier were severely wounded by an accidental grenade explosion during a Hezbollah engagement overnight. Israeli airstrikes hit Beirut's Burj al-Barajneh neighborhood and Qlaileh village. The 162nd Division is pushing deeper toward the Litani River. Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem vowed to fight 'without limits.' Senior anti-tank commander Hassan Mohammad Bashir was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
timeline_source: IDF / Times of Israel / Al Jazeera
timeline_data_point: IDF Sgt. Volansky KIA — 100+ Hezbollah rockets — woman killed Nahariya — Bashir killed

event_description: VP Vance takes lead on negotiations — Iran rejects 15-point plan — Trump extends Hormuz deadline to April 6: VP JD Vance is reportedly taking the lead role in negotiations (Axios). Iran rejected the US 15-point ceasefire proposal transmitted March 25 via Pakistan — FM Araghchi declared 'we do not plan on any negotiations.' Iran's 5-point counterproposal demands: complete halt to aggression, guarantees, reparations, comprehensive end across all fronts, Iranian sovereignty over Hormuz. Despite public rejection, Reuters notes a senior Iranian source confirmed the plan is being reviewed. Secretary Rubio told G7 allies the war could last 'another 2 to 4 weeks.' UNSC Resolution 2817 gained 135 co-sponsors.
timeline_source: Axios / Reuters / Iran International / G7 / UNSC
timeline_data_point: Vance leads negotiations — Iran rejects 15-pt plan — Rubio: '2-4 more weeks' — 135 UNSC co-sponsors

event_description: Public opinion sharply against war — 61% disapprove, 74% oppose ground troops: Pew Research (Mar 16-22, n=3,524): 61% disapprove of Trump's handling, 59% say striking Iran was wrong. Economist/YouGov: only 33% support the war. Quinnipiac: 74% oppose ground troops (including 52% of Republicans). Fox News poll: 58% oppose the war. Trump approval on Iran among independents: 24% approve / 63% disapprove (-39 net). Generic ballot (Emerson): Democrats 49% vs Republicans 42%. NCTC Director has resigned over the war. Protests continue for 27 consecutive days.
timeline_source: Pew / Economist-YouGov / Quinnipiac / Fox News / Emerson
timeline_data_point: 61% disapprove — 74% oppose ground troops — 33% support war — NCTC Director resigns

event_description: Brent $112.57 (+4.22%) — WTI briefly crosses $100 — Dow enters correction territory: Brent crude closed Friday at $112.57 (+4.22%), highest since July 2022 and up 49% in March alone — strongest monthly gain since November 1973. WTI closed at $99.64 (+5.46%) with intraday high of $100.04 — briefly crossing $100. Goldman Sachs estimates $14-18/bbl geopolitical risk premium. S&P 500 closed at 6,368.85 (-1.67%), fifth straight weekly decline. Dow 45,167 entered correction territory (>10% below recent high). VIX surged to 31.05 above 30. CNN Fear & Greed Index at 'extreme fear.'
timeline_source: ICE / NYMEX / NYSE / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs / CNN
timeline_data_point: Brent $112.57 — WTI $100.04 intraday — S&P 6,369 — VIX 31.05 — Dow correction — 'extreme fear'

event_description: Humanitarian toll approaches 3,000 killed — Iran agrees to facilitate aid through Hormuz: Iranian casualties range from 1,750 (Iran deputy UN rep) to 3,114 (HRANA) with dramatic discrepancies. HRANA documented 3,114 deaths by March 17 (1,354 civilians, 1,138 military, 622 unclassified). 217 children killed per HRANA. WHO reports 13+ hospitals/health facilities attacked, 6 evacuated. OCHA: 6,668+ civilian structures targeted. Minab school (Feb 28) killed 150-170+ (mostly schoolchildren) — UN investigating as potential war crime. In a breakthrough, Iran agreed March 27 to 'facilitate and expedite' humanitarian aid through Strait of Hormuz.
timeline_source: HRANA / WHO / OCHA / UNHRC / WaPo / Iran UN Mission
timeline_data_point: 1,750-3,114 Iranian killed — 217 children — 6,668 structures targeted — Iran allows Hormuz aid

event_description: 10 US troops injured at Prince Sultan Air Base — 300+ total US wounded: At least 10 US troops were injured in an Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia (March 27-28), which also damaged refueling aircraft. CENTCOM reports 300+ US wounded since Day 1. Total US deaths remain at 13: 6 in Kuwait drone attack (March 1), 1 from Saudi injuries (Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, 26), and 6 in KC-135 crash in western Iraq (March 12). Total US forces in region: approximately 50,000, with Trump considering 10,000 additional combat troops.
timeline_data_point: 300+ US wounded total — 10 injured Prince Sultan AB — 13 KIA — 50,000 troops deployed

event_description: Day 29 summary — Houthi entry opens third front, dual chokepoint closure threatened: Day 29 of Operation Epic Fury marks a major escalation with Houthi entry into the war. If Houthis follow through on closing Bab al-Mandeb, the dual closure of Hormuz and the Red Sea would eliminate both major Middle Eastern shipping chokepoints simultaneously — an unprecedented scenario. Markets closed the week with WTI briefly crossing $100, the Dow in correction territory, and VIX above 30. The April 6 Trump deadline for Iran to reopen Hormuz is the next critical decision point. Force posture — 82nd Airborne deploying, two ARGs converging, Bush CSG potentially en route — suggests planning for possible ground operations. Estimated cumulative cost: ~$34.3B.
timeline_data_point: ~$34.3B cumulative — Houthi third front — dual chokepoint threat — Apr 6 deadline — VIX 31 — Dow correction

event_description: BREAKING: Houthis launch first missile barrage at Israel — third active front opens: Yemen's Houthis launched their first military operation of the war — a barrage of ballistic missiles targeting 'sensitive Israeli military sites' in southern Israel. Sirens sounded in Beersheba near Israel's Dimona nuclear research center. Israel intercepted at least one missile. Houthi spokesman Brig. Gen. Yahya Saree announced the attack on Al Masirah TV. Houthi deputy information minister declared 'closing the Bab al-Mandeb strait is among our options.' Approximately 30 tankers staged near Yanbu within Houthi strike range. This ends 28 days of surprising Houthi restraint.
timeline_source: Al Masirah TV / Reuters / INSS / Soufan Center
timeline_data_point: First Houthi attack of war — Bab al-Mandeb closure threatened — 30 tankers at risk near Yanbu

event_description: Iranian missiles strike 8 sites in Tel Aviv — 1 killed, 2 injured; Kuwait airport radar damaged: On March 28, Iranian missiles struck 8 sites in Tel Aviv including a university, killing 1 and injuring 2. Iran has fired 500+ ballistic/naval missiles and ~2,000 drones since Day 1 — roughly 40% at Israel, 60% at US regional targets. Over 90% intercepted but attacks on radar systems have eroded detection network. Kuwait International Airport radar was damaged by a drone strike. Iran's missile launch rate has declined since early March — analysts cite stock depletion and rationing strategy.
timeline_source: IDF / Times of Israel / Reuters / Kuwait MoD
timeline_data_point: 500+ ballistic missiles + ~2,000 drones fired total — 90% intercepted — launch rate declining

event_description: CENTCOM: 850+ Tomahawks expended — 330 of 470 Iranian missile launchers destroyed — 92% of navy eliminated: CENTCOM Commander Adm. Brad Cooper reported 8,000+ combat flights flown and 850+ Tomahawk missiles expended in the first month. Over 10,000 Iranian military targets struck. 92% of Iran's large naval vessels destroyed — over 150 sunk — described as 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since WWII.' Israeli assessment: 330 of Iran's ~470 ballistic missile launchers destroyed or disabled, though US intelligence can only confirm destruction of roughly one-third.
timeline_data_point: 850+ Tomahawks — 150+ vessels sunk — 330/470 launchers destroyed — 8,000+ flights

event_description: Lebanon war intensifies — Division 162 deploys, Hezbollah fires 600 in 24 hours: The 2026 Lebanon war entered a critical phase. Division 162 deployed to expand the buffer zone in the western sector, joining two divisions already operating. IDF ordered destruction of all Litani River crossings. Lebanese casualties: 1,116+ killed, 3,229 injured since March 2, with 1.2 million displaced (one in five residents). IDF claims 750+ Hezbollah fighters killed. Hezbollah escalated to 600 rockets/missiles/drones in a single 24-hour period and claims 20 Israeli tanks destroyed. PM Salam banned Hezbollah military activities and expelled Iran's ambassador.
timeline_source: IDF / Lebanese MoH / Al Jazeera / Reuters
timeline_data_point: Division 162 deployed — 1,116+ Lebanese killed — 1.2M displaced — 600 Hezbollah rockets in 24 hrs

event_description: Islamabad quadrilateral summit convenes — Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Egypt seek de-escalation: Foreign ministers from Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt convened in Islamabad for a two-day quadrilateral summit (March 29-30). Originally planned for Turkey, the summit moved to Islamabad given Pakistan's deepening intermediary role. PM Shehbaz Sharif held a 90-minute call with Iranian President Pezeshkian on March 28. Germany's FM Wadephul told reporters a direct US-Iran meeting in Pakistan is expected 'very soon' — diplomatic sources suggest around March 31, potentially between Secretary Rubio and Iranian FM Araghchi.
timeline_source: Al Jazeera / Dawn / Geo News / ABC News
timeline_data_point: 4-nation summit in Islamabad — possible direct US-Iran talks ~March 31

event_description: 3,200+ 'No Kings' protests across all 50 states — Kaine-Paul war powers fails 47-53: Over 3,200 'No Kings' protests erupted across all 50 US states on March 28, the third national mobilization since the war began. Clashes reported in Los Angeles (tear gas deployed) and Dallas. The Kaine-Paul bipartisan War Powers Resolution failed 47-53 in the Senate (Rand Paul only GOP yes; Fetterman only Dem no). Multiple subsequent votes failed along identical lines. Reuters/Ipsos: only 27% approve of strikes (43% disapprove). CBS: 60%+ say administration has not provided clear explanation of war goals. Pentagon's $200 billion supplemental faces significant bipartisan resistance.
timeline_source: Reuters/Ipsos / CBS / Washington Post / CNBC / The Hill
timeline_data_point: 3,200+ protests all 50 states — 27% approve strikes — war powers fails 47-53 — $200B supplemental resisted

event_description: SPR 172M barrel exchange underway — IEA: 'worst disruption in history of global oil market': The 172 million barrel exchange from the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve is underway as part of an IEA-coordinated 400 million barrel release — the largest in IEA history with 32 countries participating. First oil shipped March 20. IEA described the disruption as the 'worst in the history of the global oil market' with Gulf supply down approximately 8 million barrels per day. Markets closed Friday with Brent at $112.57 and WTI at $99.64. Gold at approximately $4,430/oz — down ~20% from January all-time high of $5,589. 10-Year Treasury at 4.44%. Markets now price ~50% chance of a Fed rate hike by December.
timeline_source: IEA / DOE / CNBC / Yahoo Finance / FRED
timeline_data_point: SPR 172M bbl exchange — IEA 400M bbl (32 countries) — Gulf supply down ~8 mb/d — 10Y at 4.44%

event_description: Day 30 summary — One month of war: Islamabad summit, IRGC ultimatum, dual chokepoint threat: Day 30 marks one month of Operation Epic Fury. The conflict's most volatile inflection point yet features crosscutting signals: the Houthi third front threatens a twin chokepoint crisis, while diplomatic momentum builds at the Islamabad quadrilateral summit. Five flashpoints demand immediate monitoring: IRGC university ultimatum (noon March 30), Houthi Bab al-Mandeb escalation, Islamabad summit outcome, Trump's April 6 Hormuz deadline, and amphibious force convergence for potential island seizure. Estimated cumulative cost: ~$35.5 billion.
timeline_data_point: ~$35.5B cumulative — Islamabad summit — IRGC ultimatum noon Mar 30 — dual chokepoint threat — Apr 6 deadline

event_description: CENTCOM: 11,000+ targets struck — 66% of Iranian missile/drone/naval production destroyed: CENTCOM reported surpassing 11,000 struck targets across Iran since February 28, with over 66% of Iranian missile, drone, and naval production facilities destroyed per a DoD statement to Axios on March 27. IRGC-N Commander Alireza Tangsiri was confirmed killed in an Israeli strike at Bandar Abbas on March 26, along with his senior aides. The 31st MEU aboard USS Tripoli arrived in the CENTCOM AOR on March 27. The 82nd Airborne Division (1st BCT, HQ, division enablers) is deploying. Pentagon is weighing 10,000 additional combat troops.
timeline_data_point: 11,000+ targets — 66% production destroyed — 150+ vessels sunk — Tripoli operational — 82nd deploying

event_description: IRGC ultimatum: condemn university strikes by noon March 30 or face retaliatory attacks on US/Israeli campuses in Gulf: The IRGC demanded the US condemn strikes on Iranian universities by 12:00 noon March 30, threatening retaliatory attacks on US and Israeli university campuses in the Gulf region — specifically naming Texas A&M and Northwestern in Qatar, and NYU in the UAE. The ultimatum was prompted by severe damage to Iran University of Science and Technology and destruction of a water reservoir in Haftkel. IDF spokesperson Nadav Shoshani responded that Israel is 'preparing for a multi-front war.'
timeline_source: IRGC / Times of Israel / Washington Post
timeline_data_point: IRGC ultimatum: noon March 30 deadline — Texas A&M, Northwestern (Qatar), NYU (UAE) threatened

event_description: IDF strikes ~170 Hezbollah sites — 800+ Hezbollah killed — 3 journalists and 9 paramedics killed: IDF struck approximately 170 Hezbollah sites over the weekend, claiming 800+ Hezbollah operatives killed including hundreds from the elite Radwan Force. 2 Israeli soldiers were seriously wounded and 7 moderately wounded in southern Lebanon clashes. Hezbollah fired 5 rockets at Galilee. Earlier in the week, Hezbollah launched 600 rockets/missiles/drones in a single 24-hour period. 3 journalists were killed in Israeli strikes in Jezzine, including Al Manar TV's Ali Shuaib. 9 paramedics were killed on March 28 alone. Lebanese cumulative: 1,189 killed (124 children, 42 health workers), 3,229 injured, ~1 million displaced.
timeline_source: IDF / Lebanese Health Ministry / CNN / Times of Israel / WHO
timeline_data_point: ~170 Hezbollah sites struck — 1,189 Lebanese killed — 124 children — 3+ IDF divisions advancing toward Litani

event_description: Air raid sirens in Bahrain and Kuwait — Iranian attacks continue against Gulf states: Air raid sirens activated in Bahrain and Kuwait on March 29 as Iranian attacks on Gulf state targets continued. Iran has fired an estimated 500+ ballistic/naval missiles and ~2,000 drones since the war began. Iran formally closed the Strait of Hormuz to vessels going 'to and from' US, Israeli, and allied ports, while selectively opening access for China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan, Malaysia, and Thailand. Iran agreed to 20 Pakistani-flagged ships and UN humanitarian/fertilizer shipments.
timeline_data_point: Air raid sirens Bahrain + Kuwait — 500+ missiles + ~2,000 drones fired total — selective Hormuz passage

event_description: Israel strikes Arak heavy water complex and Ardakan yellowcake plant — IAEA confirms no radiological release: Israel struck the Shahid Khondab Heavy Water Complex in Arak and the Ardakan yellowcake production plant in Yazd Province, completing what IDF described as 'extensive' waves against dozens of Tehran infrastructure targets. IAEA confirmed no radiological release from either strike. Steel and cement factories across Isfahan and southern Iran were also hit. Iran University of Science and Technology and a 10,000-cubic-meter water reservoir in Haftkel, Khuzestan Province were severely damaged.
timeline_data_point: Arak heavy water + Ardakan yellowcake struck — no radiological release — university + water reservoir damaged
